elements.text: 9301.2: Preforeclosure and referral requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (Future effective date 05/01/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Preforeclosure referral account review
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Referral to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to refer a Mortgage to foreclosure – Primary Residence

elements.text: ■ When to refer a Mortgage to foreclosure for a non-Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Referral to foreclosure documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Preforeclosure referral account review
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Review of the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within 15 days prior to referral to foreclosure, the Servicer must review the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: to verify that it made every attempt to achieve quality right party contact in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Section 9102.3, and there is no approved payment arrangement or pending
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure offer for which the Borrower response period has not expired.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event that the Servicer finds that there is an approved payment arrangement or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: pending offer for an alternative to foreclosure for which the Borrower response period
elements.label: text

elements.text: has not expired, then the Servicer must not refer the Mortgage to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must document the results of its review in the Mortgage file or servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Prior approval required from Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must seek Freddie Mac’s approval to refer the Mortgage to foreclosure if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property inspections or any other information provided to the Servicer indicates that
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeding with referral may not be in Freddie Mac’s best interest or that the property is
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered a distressed property (see Section 9301.2(e) for a list of affected property
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions). See also Section 8801.4(a) regarding prior approval required from Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac for Cooperative Share Loans. Servicers must send approval requests to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosures@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Chapter 8403 for additional Servicing Contract requirements for Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages on distressed properties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Chapter 9101 for additional Servicing Contract requirements for Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages that secure Primary Residences.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Referral to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Referral to foreclosure is the submission of a Mortgage case and appropriate foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation to foreclosure counsel after taking all appropriate actions to accelerate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage. Refer to Sections 9301.2(c) and 9301.2(d) for requirements for when to refer a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) When to refer a Mortgage to foreclosure – Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must refer a Mortgage to foreclosure after expiration of the breach letter,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but no earlier than 151 days from the DDLPI (121st day of Delinquency). However,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may not refer a Mortgage to foreclosure if prohibited by applicable law or one
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the following situations applies:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is an approved payment arrangement or an alternative to foreclosure; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A complete Borrower Response Package is received and the Servicer is still within
elements.label: text

elements.text: the 30-day evaluation time period for evaluating the package as prescribed in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has extended an offer for an alternative to foreclosure and the period for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s response has not expired; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has accepted an offer for an alternative to foreclosure and is
elements.label: text

elements.text: performing in accordance with its terms; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The time period for the Borrower to exercise a right to appeal a denial of a Trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Period Plan or loan modification (per Section 9101.2) has not expired, the Servicer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: reviewing the Borrower’s appeal, or the Borrower’s time period to respond to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s decision on the appeal and any acceptance period for an alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure offered as part of that decision has not expired
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 9101.3 for additional foreclosure suspension requirements when the First
elements.label: text

elements.text: Complete Borrower Response Package is received more than 37 days prior to a scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To accept an offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower’s notification to the Servicer that he or she intends to accept an offer of an
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure may be demonstrated as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verbal notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a payment is required to legally accept an alternative to foreclosure offer, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower does not communicate a verbal or written rejection of the alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure offer, the Servicer must postpone referral to foreclosure, where legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted, at least through the last day of the month in which the first payment is due
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the terms of the alternative to foreclosure offer or until the deadline for Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptance has passed. If the Servicer receives the first payment timely in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the terms of a Trial Period Plan, repayment plan or forbearance plan, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must delay the next legal action unless the Borrower breaches the plan.

elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance

elements.label: text

elements.text: RHS or MI, if applicable.

elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of the reason for the delay in referral to foreclosure beyond 151 days from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: DDLPI (121st day of Delinquency), the Servicer’s performance will be measured against
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s State foreclosure timelines (refer to Exhibit 83, Freddie Mac State
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure Timelines).

elements.label: text

elements.text: the 30-day evaluation time period for evaluating the package as prescribed in Section
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: offer for a Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale (“short sale”) or Freddie Mac Standard
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s response has not expired; or

elements.label: text

elements.text: performing in accordance with its terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure may be demonstrated as follows:

elements.text: under the terms of the alternative to foreclosure offer or until the deadline for Borrower

elements.text: acceptance has passed. If the Servicer receives the first payment timely in accordance

elements.text: with the terms of a Trial Period Plan, the Servicer must delay the next legal action unless

elements.text: the Borrower breaches the plan.

elements.text: prior to referral to foreclosure (refer to Section 9301.2(f) for instances where Freddie

elements.text: possible to do so. The Servicer must send approval requests to

elements.text: Foreclosures@FreddieMac.com.

elements.text: (ii) Referral to foreclosure on Servicemembers or their Dependents
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act, State Military Relief Laws and Freddie Mac￾specific protections provided in Chapter 8503 limit the ability of a Servicer to refer to or
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete foreclosure on Mortgages involving Servicemembers or Dependents, as those
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms are defined in Chapter 8503. If the Servicer believes there are circumstances that
elements.label: text

elements.text: warrant an exception to these requirements, the Servicer must submit Form 105,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Multipurpose Loan Servicing Transmittal for a First-Lien Mortgage, and provide all
elements.label: text

elements.text: relevant information supporting the exception request. Refer to Chapter 8503 for further
elements.label: text

elements.text: details regarding foreclosure limitations involving Servicemembers and Dependents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Tenant-occupied properties built before 1978
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before referring a Mortgage to foreclosure on a tenant occupied property built before
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1978, if the Servicer is aware of, or becomes aware of, outstanding lead-based paint or
elements.label: text

elements.text: health code citations or violations against the property or property owner, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) and include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. A copy of the citation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Copies of documentation related to a lead-based paint violation
elements.label: text

elements.text: After reviewing the documentation, Freddie Mac will provide the Servicer with
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions on the course of action to take.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Referral to foreclosure documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Foreclosure documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appropriate foreclosure documentation includes, but is not limited to, all documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by the foreclosure counsel to complete the first legal action. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue to comply with applicable law, but at a minimum, must supply the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: counsel with the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Copies of the Note (or the original Note if required by applicable law) evidencing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: indebtedness along with any intervening assignments, endorsements, powers of
elements.label: text

elements.text: attorney or any applicable modifying instrument, such as a modification, a conversion
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement or an assumption of indebtedness and release of liability agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Mortgage or deed of trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Copy of the original title insurance policy. For Cooperative Share Loans recognized
elements.label: text

elements.text: as personal property, refer to Section 8801.1(f) regarding certain Servicer warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: required when foreclosing on a Cooperative Share Loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Copy of the breach, acceleration or demand letter sent to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Military affidavits
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Executed Substitution of Trustee, as necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Payoff statement with per diem interest as of the date of the foreclosure referral
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Send foreclosure counsel the following information by facsimile transmission or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: electronic means:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Name, mailing address and telephone number of the Borrower(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property address (if different from the Borrower’s mailing address)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A statement that the Mortgage is a Freddie Mac-owned Mortgage and includes the
elements.label: text

elements.text: nine-digit Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Name and address of the person to contact in the Servicer’s foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: department
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac needs to execute a document that is not authorized for the Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: execute under the Limited Power of Attorney (see Exhibit 53) for the Servicer to conduct
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure, the Servicer must submit the document with Form 105, Multipurpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Servicing Transmittal, to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5). Refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.3(c) for assigning Security Instruments back to the Servicer if an assignment of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security Instrument to Freddie Mac has been recorded.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Sections 8101.2(b) and 8601.2(b) for requirements on obtaining a Limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: Power of Attorney for Freddie Mac to sign documents on behalf of Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Referral to foreclosure documentation requirements on a Mortgage secured by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the above requirements, if the Mortgage is secured by a Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home, the Servicer must notify the foreclosure counsel that the property is a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home when it submits the case to the foreclosure counsel. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must also provide the foreclosure counsel with evidence that the property is legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: classified as real property under the laws in the State where the property is located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Evidence that the property is real property in a non-certificate of title State
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide the foreclosure counsel with copies of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation in non-certificate of title States (see Section 5703.4(f)(i)) evidencing that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Manufactured Home and the land are real property under the laws of the State where
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property is located:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information stating the legal basis (statutory or common law) for determining that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home is real property that is exempt from certificate of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements. This information may be included in the documentation received at
elements.label: text

elements.text: origination of the Mortgage from the title insurance company that the Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home is real property, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evidence that a certificate of title has not been issued, such as the manufacturer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: statement of origin, if the manufacturer’s statement of origin is not required to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: surrendered to a State agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Evidence that the property is real property in a certificate of title surrender State
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide the foreclosure counsel with copies of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation in certificate of title surrender States (see Section 5703.4(f)(ii))
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidencing that the Manufactured Home and the land are real property under the laws of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the State where the property is located:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A certificate of cancelation, notification letter or other acknowledgment from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) or the appropriate State agency to which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: certificate of title was surrendered, or a copy of the documents submitted in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with the surrender along with evidence that the documents were delivered
elements.label: text

elements.text: and received by the appropriate State agency, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copies of the documents, such as an affidavit of affixture, recorded in the land
elements.label: text

elements.text: records as part of the title surrender procedures to show the Manufactured Home has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been converted to real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Evidence of clear and marketable title to the Manufactured Home and land in
elements.label: text

elements.text: certificate of title States
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide the foreclosure counsel with the following documentation in
elements.label: text

elements.text: certificate of title States (see Section 5703.4(f)(iii)) evidencing the Borrower’s ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: of both the Manufactured Home and the land on which it is permanently affixed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation evidencing that the land is legally classified as real property under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: laws in the State where the property is located:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The original or a copy of the certificate of title showing the Borrower as owner of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home. The certificate of title must have a notation of the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s security interest in the Manufactured Home in the name of the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: and its successors in interest and assigns and have a notation of all intervening
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignments from the original mortgagee to each successive Servicer, ending with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: current Servicer, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the deed evidencing ownership of the land showing the owner of the land
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the deed to be identical to the owner of the Manufactured Home on the certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: This and any other relevant information must be provided within five days of the referral
elements.label: text

elements.text: to foreclosure so that the foreclosure counsel has the information necessary to
elements.label: text

elements.text: simultaneously enforce the liens (whenever possible) and so as not to unnecessarily
elements.label: text

elements.text: lengthen the foreclosure process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.3: Foreclosure process and legal considerations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Choosing a judicial or nonjudicial foreclosure process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtaining the original Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Foreclosing in the Servicer’s name
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Solicitation during the foreclosure process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer’s responsibility to work with foreclosure counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Choosing a judicial or nonjudicial foreclosure process
elements.label: text

elements.text: In States where the Servicer has the option of pursuing a judicial or nonjudicial foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: process, the Servicer must choose the nonjudicial process. However, if the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines its filing of a judicial foreclosure will preserve the right to pursue a deficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: judgment, and/or is in Freddie Mac’s best interests, the Servicer should refer to Exhibit 57A,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Approved Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement Fees and Title Expenses, to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine if Freddie Mac has approved judicial foreclosure attorney fees in the State.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac has approved judicial foreclosure attorney fees in the State, the Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursue a judicial foreclosure process without Freddie Mac’s approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac does not have approved judicial foreclosure attorney fees in the State, or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: filing of a judicial foreclosure will incur additional attorney fees exceeding the expense limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Exhibit 57A, the Servicer must obtain Freddie Mac’s approval prior to referral to judicial
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure by submitting a request for pre-approval via PAID (Payments Automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9301.6(b) regarding when to request Freddie Mac’s approval to
elements.label: text

elements.text: preserve deficiency rights.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Obtaining the original Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: If physical or constructive possession of the original Note is needed to perform the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, the Servicer must request the Note from the Document Custodian maintaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Note by submitting to the Document Custodian a completed Form 1036, Request for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Physical or Constructive Possession of Documents, or an electronic or system-generated
elements.label: text

elements.text: version of the form (or, in the case of The Bank of New York Mellon Trust Company, N.A.
elements.label: text

elements.text: as the Designated Custodian, a copy of the electronically generated 1036 Release Receipt
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report) in accordance with the requirements of Section 8107.1(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there is a full or partial reinstatement of the Mortgage, the Servicer must return the Note to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Document Custodian with either the original Form 1036 or a copy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Foreclosing in the Servicer’s name
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Conducting the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must instruct the foreclosure counsel to process the foreclosure in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s name and in a manner that would avoid any obligation to pay a transfer tax.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, the Servicer may instruct foreclosure counsel to conduct the foreclosure in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s name if applicable law:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Precludes the Servicer from conducting the foreclosure in its name because it owns or
elements.label: text

elements.text: services a subordinate Mortgage on the Mortgaged Premises, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requires the foreclosure to be processed in Freddie Mac’s name to avoid any
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligation to pay a transfer tax and foreclosure counsel could not otherwise process
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure in a manner that would successfully avoid imposition of the transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: tax obligation
elements.label: text

elements.text: For these special circumstances, the Servicer does not need to obtain written approval but
elements.label: text

elements.text: must notify Freddie Mac within two Business Days of the Servicer’s determination to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclose in Freddie Mac’s name and record the basis of the decision in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All notifications must be sent via e-mail (see Directory 5). For all other circumstances in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the Servicer may need to instruct foreclosure counsel to conduct the foreclosure in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s name, the Servicer must obtain written approval from Freddie Mac (refer
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Section 9402.1(b) regarding initiating legal actions on Freddie Mac’s behalf).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When processing the foreclosure in Freddie Mac’s name, all pleadings and related
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents must comply with Section 9402.1(b)(iii). The Servicer remains obligated to
elements.label: text

elements.text: notify Freddie Mac pursuant to Section 9501.5(d) in the event that any foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: conducted in Freddie Mac’s name evolves into a non-routine litigation matter (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9402.1(b)).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Servicer conducts the foreclosure in Freddie Mac’s name, the Servicer is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted to have the same foreclosure counsel represent the Servicer or another
elements.label: text

elements.text: lienholder in the same proceeding. Freddie Mac does not consent to dual representation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Freddie Mac and another lienholder on the same property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Executing documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac needs to execute a document that is not authorized for the Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: execute under the Limited Power of Attorney (see Exhibit 53, Limited Power of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Attorney) for the Servicer to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Process the foreclosure, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Execute a document related to a foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must submit Form 105, Multipurpose Loan Servicing Transmittal, to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) with all supporting documentation, which may include,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but is not limited to, the last recorded document in the chain of title, and include the
elements.label: text

elements.text: document Freddie Mac needs to execute.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Sections 8101.2(b) and 8601.2(b) for requirements on obtaining a Limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: Power of Attorney for Freddie Mac to sign documents on behalf of Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If an assignment of the Security Instrument to Freddie Mac has been recorded and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is conducting the foreclosure in its name, then the Security Instrument must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: assigned back to the Servicer before the foreclosure counsel files the first legal action.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 9301.5(a)(i) for an explanation of first legal action.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To have the Security Instrument assigned back to the Servicer, the Servicer must submit a
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed assignment with a Request for Assistance Form (available at:
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/docs/pdf/fact-sheet/sats699.pdf), to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 9). Freddie Mac will endeavor to execute the assignment and return it to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer within 10-12 Business Days of receiving the documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is foreclosing on a Mortgage registered with MERS®, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepare and execute (using the Servicer’s employee who is a MERS authorized “signing
elements.label: text

elements.text: officer”) an assignment of the Security Instrument from MERS to the Servicer. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must record the prepared assignment where required by State law. State
elements.label: text

elements.text: mandated recordings are non-reimbursable by Freddie Mac, are not considered part of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac allowable foreclosure counsel fees and must not be billed to the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is an FHA, Section 502 GRH or VA Mortgage, then the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: follow FHA, RHS or VA guidelines to determine whose name the foreclosure action
elements.label: text

elements.text: should be brought.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 6301.2(d) for additional information on Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for assignments of the Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Solicitation during the foreclosure process
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer previously determined that a Borrower, who was less than 60 days delinquent at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the time of evaluation, did not qualify for any alternative to foreclosure and the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsequently becomes 60 days delinquent, then the Servicer must continue its solicitation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and collection efforts with the Borrower in accordance with the requirements of Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9102. Continued solicitation includes sending another Borrower Solicitation Package, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, if documentation needs to be refreshed in order to perform the subsequent
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation of the Borrower for alternatives to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless required to continue contact attempts by applicable law, attempts to contact any
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent Borrower and related loss mitigation solicitations must discontinue 60 days prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a foreclosure sale date for judicial foreclosures or 30 days prior to a foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: for non-judicial foreclosures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loss mitigation solicitations while the Mortgage is in foreclosure must be communicated to
elements.label: text

elements.text: and coordinated with the foreclosure counsel, as appropriate. A Servicer must keep the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure counsel informed of the status of relevant alternative to foreclosure negotiations
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must notify the foreclosure counsel within two Business Days after arrangements for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure have been agreed to or within two Business Days after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is fully reinstated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Servicer’s responsibility to work with foreclosure counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) After Servicer referral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the Servicer has referred a Mortgage to foreclosure, the Servicer must work with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure counsel to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Identify any viable alternatives to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Monitor the progress of the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Facilitate prompt and efficient completion of the foreclosure proceedings and
elements.label: text

elements.text: acquisition of clear and marketable title, including conducting the foreclosure in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: way that will expedite an eviction of the tenant or Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Communication
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must maintain communication with the foreclosure counsel so that the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: can manage the foreclosure effectively. No less than one time per month, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must require the foreclosure counsel to report the status of the foreclosure, request any
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation needed from Servicer and report any relevant information to the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain this information in the Mortgage file or on its Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Compliance with the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (SCRA)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must ensure foreclosure counsel complies with the SCRA and all State
elements.label: text

elements.text: Military Relief Laws as they apply to any Borrower or Mortgage including verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the Borrower is not a Servicemember currently serving a Period of Military Service
elements.label: text

elements.text: (as those terms are referenced in Section 8503.1(a)), or if the Borrower was a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicemember, that more than one year has elapsed since their period of Military
elements.label: text

elements.text: Service ended. (See Section 9701.3(a) regarding when verification should be completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: by foreclosure counsel).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Providing information to the foreclosure counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any Mortgage that the Servicer refers for foreclosure, the Servicer must provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete written reinstatement or payoff figures to foreclosure counsel, workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: specialist or outsourcing vendor requesting the information. This information must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided within two Business Days of the date on which a written request is received.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may provide the written reinstatement or payoff figures via a paper
elements.label: text

elements.text: document, facsimile or e-mail.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the foreclosure counsel requests additional information and/or documentation from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer (such as certificates of judgment), at any time, the Servicer must provide such
elements.label: text

elements.text: requested information and/or documents within three Business Days after receipt of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: request, or within such earlier time frame if necessary to comply with timing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements under applicable law or court orders and procedures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Foreclosure counsel fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure counsel fees and costs must be reasonable and comparable to those
elements.label: text

elements.text: customarily charged in the area where the property is located. Exhibit 57A, Approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement Fees and Title Expenses, provides
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s expense limits for foreclosure counsel fees. The Servicer must obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s written approval prior to incurring foreclosure counsel fees in excess of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the expense limits. Refer to Section 9701.1(c) for details on obtaining Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: written pre-approval for exceeding the expense limits in Exhibit 57A.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Fees and Borrower’s defenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for Freddie Mac’s proportionate share of
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses for responding to Borrower defenses. Refer to Section 9402.1(b) and Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9701 for details regarding expense reimbursements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.4: Reporting requirements: referral and during
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: the foreclosure proceeding (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly foreclosure reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Responding to and reporting Borrower defenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting the scheduled foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via an EDR transmission within the first three Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days of the month following the month that the Servicer referred the Mortgage to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Use a default action code of 43 and provide the date the Mortgage was referred to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For additional information on EDR requirements, refer to Section 9102.6(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Monthly foreclosure reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the Servicer has reported that a Mortgage has been referred to foreclosure the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must report a default action code 68 (Date of first legal action) with the corresponding date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the first legal action (as defined in Section 9301.5(a)(i)). The Servicer must continue to report
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the Mortgage is in foreclosure in its monthly EDR transmission using a default action
elements.label: text

elements.text: code of 43 (Referred to foreclosure) until:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer reports the results of a foreclosure sale or the execution of a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer reports that the Mortgage is fully reinstated or paid off, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A workout is completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: For additional information on EDR requirements, refer to Section 9102.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Responding to and reporting Borrower defenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Responding to Borrower defenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must respond to legal actions brought by the Borrower during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure. Refer to Chapter 9401 for specific requirements for handling bankruptcy and
elements.label: text

elements.text: routine litigation and Chapter 9402 for non-routine litigation actions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Reporting a bankruptcy filing
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower files bankruptcy during the foreclosure process, the Servicer must report
elements.label: text

elements.text: the bankruptcy filing to Freddie Mac. The Servicer must submit this notification via an
elements.label: text

elements.text: EDR transmission within the first three Business Days of the month following the month
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the bankruptcy was filed. The Servicer must include the date of the filing and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable default action code to indicate the bankruptcy chapter number, as shown
elements.label: text

elements.text: below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bankruptcy default action codes
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bankruptcy Chapter Default Action Code
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 12 bankruptcy petition filed 59
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 7 bankruptcy petition filed 65
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 11 bankruptcy petition filed 66
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 13 bankruptcy petition filed 67
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must also notify Freddie Mac when the bankruptcy plan is confirmed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting a default action code of 69 (Bankruptcy plan confirmed), and providing the date
elements.label: text

elements.text: the plan was confirmed, or in the event of a scheduled bankruptcy cramdown, by
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting a default action code of 35 (bankruptcy cramdown Scheduled). The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must continue to report each month that the Borrower is in bankruptcy until the
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy is cleared or the stay lifted, and the Servicer has reported the event to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the bankruptcy is cleared or the stay is lifted, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: via an EDR transmission within the first three Business days of the month following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month that the action occurred. Use a default action code of 76 (Bankruptcy court
elements.label: text

elements.text: clearance obtained/stay lifted) and provide the date that the action occurred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For additional information on EDR requirements refer to Section 9102.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Reporting a contested foreclosure and/or other litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of a contested foreclosure. The Servicer must also
elements.label: text

elements.text: notify Freddie Mac of any pending litigation that affects the enforceability of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or the marketability of the property securing the Mortgage. (Refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9402.1(b).) The Servicer must submit this notification via an EDR transmission within
elements.label: text

elements.text: the first three Business Days of the month following the month in which the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: became aware of the contested foreclosure or other litigation. Use a default action code of
elements.label: text

elements.text: 33 (Contested foreclosure and litigation) and provide the date that the action occurred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report each month that the foreclosure is being contested or about the
elements.label: text

elements.text: other litigation as long as the case is pending.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For additional information on EDR requirements, refer to Section 9102.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Reporting the scheduled foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via an EDR transmission within the first three
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Days of the month following the month that a foreclosure sale has been scheduled,
elements.label: text

elements.text: by reporting a default action code of 71 (Foreclosure sale scheduled) along with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: scheduled sale date. When the Servicer reports the scheduled foreclosure sale in its EDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: transmission, the Servicer must also indicate that the Mortgage is in foreclosure using a
elements.label: text

elements.text: default action code of 43 (Referred to foreclosure). Therefore, the Servicer will be reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: multiple default action codes in the same EDR transmission.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For additional information on EDR requirements, refer to Section 9102.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.5: First legal action and expedited foreclosure
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ First legal action in the foreclosure and expedited process for vacant or abandoned properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expedited foreclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expedited foreclosures – Florida
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expedited foreclosures – Illinois
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expedited foreclosures – New York
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) First legal action in the foreclosure and expedited process for vacant or abandoned
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) What is the first legal action in the foreclosure process?
elements.label: text

elements.text: The first legal action is the first public action required in the jurisdiction where the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property is located to commence the foreclosure process. The first legal action may
elements.label: text

elements.text: include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For judicial foreclosures, a complaint, petition, order to docket or notice of hearing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For non-judicial foreclosures, recording a notice of default or publication of a notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: of sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For foreclosures other than judicial and non-judicial foreclosures, if any, the earliest
elements.label: text

elements.text: document that establishes, sets or schedules a date for a foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: This first legal action must take place after the referral to foreclosure. Refer to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following Sections for more information:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional information supporting first legal action in the foreclosure process
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Referral to foreclosure Section 9301.2(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: When to refer a Mortgage to foreclosure – Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9301.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: When to refer a Mortgage to foreclosure for a non-Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9301.2(d)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Referral to foreclosure documentation requirements Section 9301.2(f)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Expedited foreclosure if property is vacant or abandoned
elements.label: text

elements.text: Some jurisdictions permit an expedited or alternative foreclosure process if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises is vacant or abandoned. If the Servicer decides it is in Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: best interest to pursue an expedited foreclosure as a result of the occupancy status of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises, then the Servicer may proceed with an expedited foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requesting additional attorney fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer believes that pursuing an expedited foreclosure is in Freddie Mac’s best
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest and knows that additional attorney fees will be incurred when processing this
elements.label: text

elements.text: expedited foreclosure, the Servicer must submit a request for pre-approval to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac via PAID (Payments Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tools) to recommend incurring additional attorney fees to complete the expedited
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure. The Servicer must send the recommendation to Freddie Mac before the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer incurs additional fees; otherwise, Freddie Mac will not reimburse for those fees.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under no circumstances should the Servicer delay initiating foreclosure to obtain Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s approval to pursue an expedited foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Expedited foreclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must always consider how to resolve a Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter in a way
elements.label: text

elements.text: that obtains the best result for Freddie Mac and in a legally-compliant manner. While
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are not required to use expedited methods identified in this chapter, if a Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: does proceed with an expedited method, it must adhere to the requirements set forth in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter provides requirements for expediting Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters, such
elements.label: text

elements.text: as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Waiving Freddie Mac’s right to pursue deficiency actions against a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expediting the foreclosure if the property is identified as vacant/abandoned
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtaining default judgment for an amount less than total amount owed by the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ State-specific methods for expediting Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Expedited foreclosures – Florida
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Determining the judgment method
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the State of Florida, Servicers may obtain judgment in connection with a foreclosure in
elements.label: text

elements.text: several ways, including the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Motion for summary judgment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bulk trial foreclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Order to show cause
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtaining judgment for less than the total indebtedness as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure timeline requirements set forth in Section 9301.11(a) will not be waived
elements.label: text

elements.text: regardless of the procedure the Servicer follows when foreclosing the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Motion for summary judgment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may move for summary judgment against the Borrower using the expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: limits set forth in Exhibit 57A, Approved Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Postponement Fees and Title Expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Bulk trial foreclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may utilize bulk trial foreclosures to accelerate foreclosures. This option can be
elements.label: text

elements.text: used for Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters that were:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Referred to law firms on or after June 1, 2013 or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Referred to law firms before June 1, 2013 and for which the foreclosure complaint
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been filed, but final judgment of foreclosure has not yet been entered
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers and their counsel must use their discretion to determine whether the bulk trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure process is appropriate for the jurisdiction, the Mortgage and the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises that is the subject of the foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reimbursement of expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pursuant to Section 9701.3(a), Freddie Mac will reimburse Servicers for approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: attorney fees as set forth in Exhibit 57A. Servicers must submit expenses for additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: attorney fees associated with the use of the bulk trial foreclosure process through PAID
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools). Freddie Mac will reimburse up to a maximum of
elements.label: text

elements.text: $1,750, including legal fees for all necessary work to complete bulk trial foreclosures,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identification of bulk trial foreclosure candidates
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pre-trial preparation and correspondence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Preparation of trial orders
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Witness preparation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All necessary court appearances
elements.label: text

elements.text: This fee is in addition to the foreclosure attorney fee. Servicers must use expense code
elements.label: text

elements.text: 010009 (Bulk Trial Foreclosure/Inquest Process) when submitting an expense in PAID
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) for the additional trial fees associated with the bulk trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure process. Legal expenses paid by the Servicer must match or exceed the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Order to show cause
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may foreclose using motions for order to show cause against the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to the expense limits set forth in Exhibit 57A.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Default judgment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may move for default judgment against the Borrower in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 9301.6(a)(i) and 9301.6(a)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Expedited foreclosures – Illinois
elements.label: text

elements.text: In Illinois, Servicers may obtain a consent judgment from a Borrower and take title to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises on behalf of Freddie Mac instead of selling the Mortgaged Premises at
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must comply with the requirements in Section 9301.1(b) and applicable law prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to obtaining a consent judgment pursuant to this section. Servicers may also offer a
elements.label: text

elements.text: relocation incentive to a qualified Borrower as described in this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises must be located in Illinois
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicers may seek reimbursement from Freddie Mac, subject to the expense limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: set forth in Exhibit 57A, Approved Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fees and Title Expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payments from Freddie Mac or the Borrower to subordinate lienholders, if any, are
elements.label: text

elements.text: not permitted. Most subordinate liens will be extinguished by operation of law once
elements.label: text

elements.text: the consent judgment is final.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Relocation incentive requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may pay a relocation incentive of up to $3,000 to the Borrower in a manner
elements.label: text

elements.text: similar to the relocation assistance for completion of a Freddie Mac Deed-in-Lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure (“deed-in-lieu of foreclosure”) if the Borrower occupies the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises as his or her Primary Residence at the time the Borrower consents to judgment
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. If the Borrower receives relocation assistance from a source other than Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: or the Servicer, such as an employer, the Servicer must reduce the amount of Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s relocation assistance by the amount received from the other source. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower will receive relocation assistance from a source other than Freddie Mac or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer and the amount is equal to or greater than $3,000, Freddie Mac will not
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide any relocation assistance to the extent that the Servicer is aware of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Borrowers subject to Permanent Change of Station (PCS) orders will not be eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Freddie Mac relocation assistance as the government assists in defraying the costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the service member’s relocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Servicer must conduct an interior inspection of the Mortgaged Premises to ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: that it is vacant, undamaged and in broom-swept condition in the same manner and
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the same requirements as stated in Section 9209.5(d) for a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, unless the property is a 2- to 4-unit property and Freddie Mac allows
elements.label: text

elements.text: tenants to remain in one or more unit(s); in which case, the Servicer must ensure the
elements.label: text

elements.text: remaining units are vacant, undamaged and in broom-swept condition. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must take all necessary actions to protect the Mortgaged Premises from waste,
elements.label: text

elements.text: damage and vandalism.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the property inspection reveals any environmental hazards or legal concerns as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 9209.1(c), the Servicer must contact Freddie Mac for
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval prior to obtaining the consent judgment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the property inspection indicates that the Mortgaged Premises was not left in
elements.label: text

elements.text: broom-swept condition or there is damage to the Mortgaged Premises, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must proceed to judgment and do all of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Estimate the costs for remediating the issues
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Reduce the Borrower relocation assistance by the estimated amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Secure the property following the requirements in Section 9603.1(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for the actual cost of a final property
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspection conducted by an outside service up to the expense limits stated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 57, 1- to 4-Unit Property Approved Expense Amounts. Refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9701.2(d) for details regarding reimbursement of property inspection costs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Once the final amount of relocation assistance has been determined, the consent
elements.label: text

elements.text: judgment has been obtained and the Mortgaged Premises have been inspected and
elements.label: text

elements.text: found to be in acceptable condition, the Servicer must disburse to the Borrower the
elements.label: text

elements.text: relocation assistance payment. The Servicer must distribute the relocation assistance
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds no more than 30 days after entry of judgment. The Servicer may request
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement for the relocation assistance from Freddie Mac via PAID (see Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 88, Servicing Tools.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. In addition to the relocation assistance offered by Freddie Mac, the Servicer may in
elements.label: text

elements.text: its discretion offer the Borrower an additional financial incentive to obtain the
elements.label: text

elements.text: consent of the Borrower to entry of judgment. Any relocation assistance provided by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer will be in addition to the relocation assistance amount provided by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and the Servicer’s assistance amount does not have to be subtracted
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the assistance amount Freddie Mac will pay. If the Servicer does offer its own
elements.label: text

elements.text: relocation assistance, it must provide the assistance from its own funds, as a payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: made directly to the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. The relocation assistance payment may not be applied to other debts secured by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises or retained by the Servicer for any reason
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Foreclosure sale reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report the Mortgage as reverting to REO through a foreclosure sale,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and not a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. The Servicer must report the “sale results” as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount of the judgment (as there is no foreclosure sale) and the Servicer must report the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “sale date” as the date the judgment is entered.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer also waives the deficiency in connection with obtaining the consent
elements.label: text

elements.text: judgment, then the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac that the deficiency rights have not
elements.label: text

elements.text: been preserved at the time the foreclosure sales results are being reported as provided in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9301.9(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Expedited foreclosures – New York
elements.label: text

elements.text: In New York, certain counties or courts have established guidelines for an alternative course
elements.label: text

elements.text: of legal action, the New York Foreclosure Inquest Program and/or bulk trials, as a method to
elements.label: text

elements.text: expedite foreclosure actions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may utilize the New York Foreclosure Inquest Program and/or bulk trials as an
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative foreclosure process to accelerate Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters in New
elements.label: text

elements.text: York. Servicers and their counsel must use their discretion to determine if the use of the New
elements.label: text

elements.text: York Foreclosure Inquest Program and/or bulk trials is in Freddie Mac’s best interest based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the local jurisdiction, the Mortgage and the Mortgaged Premises that is the subject of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, and if the foreclosure action meets the eligibility criteria for inclusion in the New
elements.label: text

elements.text: York Foreclosure Inquest Program as determined by the participating county or judge.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure timeline requirements set forth in Section 9301.11(a) will not be waived as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: result of using the New York Foreclosure Inquest Program and/or bulk trials and will remain
elements.label: text

elements.text: in effect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reimbursement of expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pursuant to Section 9701.3(a), Freddie Mac will reimburse Servicers for approved attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees as set forth in Exhibit 57A, Approved Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fees and Title Expenses. Servicers must submit expenses for additional attorney fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: associated with the use of the New York Foreclosure Inquest Program and/or bulk trials
elements.label: text

elements.text: through PAID (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools). Freddie Mac will reimburse up to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum of $1,750, which is the maximum legal fee for all work necessary to complete an
elements.label: text

elements.text: inquest, including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identification of Foreclosure Inquest Program candidates
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pre-trial preparation and correspondence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Preparation of trial orders
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Witness preparation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All necessary court appearances
elements.label: text

elements.text: This fee is in addition to the approved foreclosure attorney fee. Servicers must use expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: code 010009 (Bulk Trial Foreclosure/Inquest Process) when submitting a claim request in
elements.label: text

elements.text: PAID for the additional trial fees associated with a Mortgage proceeding in the New York
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure Inquest Program. Legal expenses paid by the Servicer must match or exceed the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.6: Deficiency rights and property considerations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtaining judgment for less than total indebtedness and preserving deficiency rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Circumstances where preserving deficiency rights requires additional expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower abandonment of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Preserving the property during the foreclosure process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expenses that may become First Liens on the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Obtaining judgment for less than total indebtedness and preserving deficiency rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Obtaining judgment for less than total indebtedness
elements.label: text

elements.text: While Servicers are required to obtain judgment against the Borrower for the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: indebtedness due under the Note and Mortgage in accordance with applicable law and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide, there may be cases where the foreclosure proceeding will be significantly
elements.label: text

elements.text: shortened if the Servicer only seeks judgment for any amount less than the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: indebtedness (e.g., for only principal and interest owed by the Borrower). If the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines that obtaining judgment for any amount less than the total indebtedness is in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s best interest, it may do so. The Servicer must record the decision to obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: judgment for a lesser amount and the grounds for its decision in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to obtaining final judgment of foreclosure on any amount less than the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: indebtedness, the Servicer must ensure that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All requirements of the Guide have been met
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The ratio of the sum of principal and interest to the market value of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is 100% or more. The Servicer must obtain the value of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises in accordance with Section 9202.4(a), as if the Servicer were obtaining an
elements.label: text

elements.text: “estimated market value” of the Mortgaged Premises for a short sale (see Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9208.2(a)(ii) for instructions on obtaining a short sale property value.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In the event the Borrower reinstates or satisfies the Mortgage after judgment is
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained, the Servicer must instruct foreclosure counsel to vacate the judgment. If this
elements.label: text

elements.text: is not possible and the Servicer has unreimbursed expenses or advances that are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: collectable because it obtained judgment for an amount less than the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: indebtedness, the Servicer may not seek reimbursement from Freddie Mac for those
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses or advances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) When to preserve/waive deficiency rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to preserve deficiency rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: In all instances, except as set forth in this section, where the foreclosure timeline will
elements.label: text

elements.text: not be extended or additional fees/costs will not be incurred above the approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: expense limits set forth in Exhibit 57A, Approved Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Postponement Fees and Title Expenses, Servicers must work with their foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: attorney to preserve Freddie Mac’s right to pursue a deficiency action. Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: reserves the right to pursue deficiencies after the foreclosure sale on a case-by-case
elements.label: text

elements.text: basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage has mortgage insurance, the Servicer must follow the MI’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions for pursuing deficiency judgments or waiving deficiency judgments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to waive deficiency rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must adhere to the requirements provided in this section in order to
elements.label: text

elements.text: waive Freddie Mac’s right to pursue a deficiency judgment against a Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In many jurisdictions, applicable law provides Freddie Mac the right to pursue a
elements.label: text

elements.text: deficiency balance after a foreclosure sale. In some instances, it may be in Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s best interest to waive the right to collect the deficiency including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, cases where a waiver will do one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Expedite the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Expeditiously resolve litigation and/or bankruptcy in Freddie Mac’s favor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Result in a consent judgment conveying the Mortgaged Premises to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Illinois
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Obtain a Borrower’s consent to the final judgment of foreclosure on an expedited
elements.label: text

elements.text: basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Expedite or result in the entry of an order confirming or ratifying the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale by the court
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Minimize or avoid the imposition of additional redemption, confirmation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratification periods, if State law would impose such additional periods
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that waiving Freddie Mac’s right to pursue a deficiency is
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Freddie Mac’s best interest, then the Servicer may use its discretion without
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtaining Freddie Mac’s prior approval to waive Freddie Mac’s right to pursue a
elements.label: text

elements.text: deficiency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer waives Freddie Mac’s right to pursue a deficiency, then at the time it
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports the foreclosure sale results as required by Section 9301.9(a), the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: notify Freddie Mac that the deficiency rights have not been preserved.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must record that it waived Freddie Mac’s deficiency rights, as well as
elements.label: text

elements.text: the grounds for its decision, in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must meet the requirements of Section 9301.9(a) when waiving deficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: rights.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Circumstances where preserving deficiency rights requires additional expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer knows that additional attorney fees will be incurred when preserving Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s right to pursue a deficiency action, and the Servicer believes it is in Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: best interest to preserve Freddie Mac’s right to pursue a deficiency action against the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, the Servicer must submit a request for pre-approval (RPA) to Freddie Mac via
elements.label: text

elements.text: PAID (Payments Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) to
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommend incurring additional attorney fees to perfect Freddie Mac’s rights to pursue a
elements.label: text

elements.text: deficiency action. If possible, the Servicer should send the recommendation to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Servicer refers a Mortgage to foreclosure. However, under no circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: should the Servicer delay referring a Mortgage to foreclosure to obtain Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval to preserve Freddie Mac’s deficiency rights. When submitting a recommendation to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, the Servicer must follow the instructions provided below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must only send a recommendation to Freddie Mac if the Servicer or its attorney is
elements.label: text

elements.text: able to provide justification that would outweigh both the delays in the foreclosure process,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the increased fees and costs. Servicers should include an explanation for the request in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the RPA comment field along with the electronic attachment of any supporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers will not receive foreclosure timeline compensatory fee consideration for delaying
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure process unless explicitly stated by Freddie Mac in its response to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s recommendation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all other issues related to preserving deficiency rights where additional expenses are
elements.label: text

elements.text: required, Servicers should direct their questions to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Borrower abandonment of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Preservation of deficiency rights should be sought against a Borrower who has abandoned
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property. When these cases fall outside of the requirements set forth in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.6(a)(ii), it is the Servicer’s responsibility to send a recommendation to Freddie Mac by
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitting a request for pre-approval to Freddie Mac within five Business Days of
elements.label: text

elements.text: discovering the property is abandoned.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all other issues related to abandonment as it relates to the preservation of deficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: rights, Servicers should direct their questions to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Preserving the property during the foreclosure process
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must take the following actions to preserve and maintain the property during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure process:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Ensure the property is covered by property insurance throughout the foreclosure process,
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with the requirements of Section 9603.1(d). This includes taking all actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: required in the mortgage clause of all applicable property insurance policies, including,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but not limited to, providing all notices to the insurer required under such clause, in order
elements.label: text

elements.text: to preserve the coverage and its maximum benefits for the Servicer and/or Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as mortgagee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Take all necessary steps to protect the property from waste, damage and vandalism, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: winterize, as appropriate (see Exhibit 57, 1- to 4-Unit Property Approved Expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amounts, for an outline of winterization requirements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Obtain approval from Freddie Mac for any property preservation expenses that either
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceed Freddie Mac’s approval limits or will be incurred after the date of a foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale, including where the property is sold to a third party by submitting a request for pre￾approval of expenses (RPA) to Freddie Mac via PAID (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Refer to Section 9701.2(d) regarding reimbursement for property preservation
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses.)
elements.label: text
