elements.text: 3. Comply with any property preservation requirements of the FHA, RHS, VA or MI, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. For leasehold Mortgages, ensure that payments required under the terms of the lease (i.e.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ground rents) are made to prevent termination of the lease, if termination of the lease will
elements.label: text

elements.text: impair Freddie Mac’s lien position or interest in the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting lead-based paint violations
elements.label: text

elements.text: If during the foreclosure process the Servicer becomes aware of lead-based paint violations
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the property, then the Servicer must report such violations to Freddie Mac (see Directory
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5) within five Business Days of learning of the violation. The Servicer must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the citation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copies of documentation related to a lead-based paint violation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Expenses that may become First Liens on the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain bills and make payment for all expenses requiring payment under
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Security Instrument. Such expenses may include, but are not limited to, property taxes,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ground rents, and assessments or other charges (e.g., utility assessments,
elements.label: text

elements.text: condominium/homeowners association (HOA) or Cooperative Corporation assessments, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Unit maintenance fees or Cooperative Unit Maintenance Fees) that, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent, are or may become First Liens priority on the property or that if not paid would
elements.label: text

elements.text: result in the subordination of Freddie Mac’s interests. (See Section 8801.2(c) regarding
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loan expenses that may become First Liens on the property.) If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Escrow Funds are insufficient to pay these items as they become due during
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, or, if there is no Escrow account or if the Escrow account does not hold funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: for these expenses, the Servicer must advance funds to pay these expenses, when and to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: extent necessary, to protect Freddie Mac’s interests.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Servicer must contact Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) and obtain Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s written approval before making payments to taxing authorities when federal, State or
elements.label: text

elements.text: local income tax liens would take priority over Freddie Mac’s First Lien position.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.7: Complete Borrower Response Packages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: received after referral to foreclosure and other actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to foreclosure sale (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complete Borrower Response Packages received after referral to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reviews and certifications prior to foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Foreclosure sale date extensions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Complete Borrower Response Packages received after referral to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) First Complete Borrower Response Package received after referral to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: and greater than 37 days prior to the foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 9101.3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) All other Borrower Response Packages received after referral to foreclosure and
elements.label: text

elements.text: greater than 37 days prior to the foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer receives a complete Borrower Response Package after referral to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, the Servicer is not required to suspend foreclosure proceedings to review the
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete Borrower Response Package and notify the Borrower of the decision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To accept an offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower’s notification to the Servicer that he or she intends to accept an offer may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: demonstrated as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verbal notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a payment is required to legally accept an alternative to foreclosure offer, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower does not communicate a verbal or written rejection of the alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure offer, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Postpone foreclosure actions where legally permitted at least through the last day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the month that the first payment is due under the terms of the alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure offer or until the deadline for Borrower acceptance has passed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delay the next legal action if the Servicer receives the first payment timely in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the terms of a Trial Period Plan, repayment plan or forbearance plan,
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless the Borrower breaches the plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event a foreclosure sale occurs, the Servicer’s performance will be measured
elements.label: text

elements.text: against Freddie Mac’s State foreclosure timelines without consideration given to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s postponement of the foreclosure sale (refer to Exhibit 83, Freddie Mac State
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure Timelines).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Response Package received without a short sale purchase offer and
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale date is scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the foreclosure sale has been scheduled and the Servicer sends the Borrower a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Evaluation Notice that is an intent to pursue a short sale based on receipt of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete Borrower Response Package, but a purchase offer was not received, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Evaluation Notice must be amended to indicate the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contact a real estate broker to list your property for sale if it is not currently listed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Once you receive a potential buyer’s offer, contact us immediately and we will
elements.label: text

elements.text: review the offer to determine whether to accept it in full or partial satisfaction of your
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Short sale evaluations
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the Mortgage has been referred to foreclosure, the Servicer must continue with
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure proceedings during the short sale process, except where delay for motion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: judgment is provided for Mortgages subject to judicial proceedings. However, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must not complete a foreclosure sale if the short sale has been approved based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on a review of a purchase offer and a complete Borrower Response Package (or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: streamlined documentation permitted in Section 9208.1(c)). The Servicer must suspend
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure sale where permitted under State or local law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deed-in-lieu of foreclosure evaluations
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the Mortgage has been referred to foreclosure, the Servicer must continue with
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure proceedings during the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure process, except where
elements.label: text

elements.text: delay for motion of judgment is provided for Mortgages subject to judicial proceedings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may only suspend a foreclosure sale after the executed deed and all required
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed-in-lieu of foreclosure documents have been received by the Servicer. Additionally,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must receive an executed deed from the Borrower no later than 30 days prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the scheduled foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When considering a Borrower for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the Servicer must ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: that there is sufficient time to complete processing of the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: (inclusive of sending the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure offer to the Borrower) so that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer receives the executed deed no later than 30 days prior to the foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Borrower Response Package received 37-15 days prior to the scheduled foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a complete Borrower Response Package (including a short sale purchase offer, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable) is submitted between 37 and 15 days before a scheduled foreclosure sale date,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must expedite its review and make a determination regarding the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: request for assistance at least seven days before the scheduled foreclosure sale date. This
elements.label: text

elements.text: will ensure that the Servicer will be able to determine whether it must send a foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification to the foreclosure counsel at least seven, but no more than 15 days prior to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: scheduled foreclosure sale date, if appropriate or offer the Borrower an alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a short sale transaction, if the Servicer has approved the short sale based on a review
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a purchase offer and a complete Borrower Response Package (or other streamlined
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation as permitted), the Servicer must suspend the foreclosure sale where
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted under State or local law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer sends the Borrower a Borrower Evaluation Notice that is an intent to
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursue a Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale based on receipt of a complete Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package, but a purchase offer was not received, then the Borrower Evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notice must be amended to indicate the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contact a real estate broker to list your property for sale if it is not currently listed. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: your property is not currently listed, there may not be adequate time to market the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property or to review a potential buyer’s offer prior to the foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Once you receive a potential buyer’s offer, contact us immediately and we will
elements.label: text

elements.text: review the offer to determine whether to accept it in full or partial satisfaction of your
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The “Standard Short Sale – All Other Scenarios” model clause included in Exhibit 93,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evaluation Notices, is representative of this Borrower Evaluation Notice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Borrower Response Package received less than 15 days prior to the scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a complete Borrower Response Package is received less than 15 days prior to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: scheduled foreclosure sale date, the Servicer must first determine whether it can conduct
elements.label: text

elements.text: an expedited review of the Borrower Response Package and render a decision by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: latest foreclosure certification date (i.e., seven days prior to the scheduled foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale date) and if so, the Servicer must complete the review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer offers the Borrower a home retention alternative to foreclosure, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expiration of the 14-day Borrower response period will occur on or after a scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale date, it must not provide the certification to the foreclosure counsel and
elements.label: text

elements.text: must make every effort to suspend the foreclosure sale for up to 14 days where legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted under State or local law
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 9301.7(a) for foreclosure suspension requirements related to short
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is not in violation of this requirement if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A court with jurisdiction over the foreclosure proceeding (if any), or the bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: court in a bankruptcy case, or the public official charged with carrying out the activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: or event, fails or refuses to halt some or all activities or events in the matter after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer has made reasonable efforts to move the court or request the public official
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a cessation of the activity or event
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has taken action to protect the interests of Freddie Mac in response to
elements.label: text

elements.text: action taken by the Borrower or other parties in the foreclosure process (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy filed by the Borrower, situations where the Borrower files a motion that
elements.label: text

elements.text: requires a response, or similar situations)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must document in the Mortgage file if any of the foregoing exceptions to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirement to halt an existing foreclosure action are applicable. In the event a
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale occurs, the Servicer’s performance will be measured against Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s State foreclosure timelines without consideration given to the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: postponement of the foreclosure sale (refer to Exhibit 83).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Solely for the purpose of suspending foreclosure sale, a Borrower’s notification to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer that he or she intends to accept an offer may be demonstrated as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remittance of a payment due under an alternative to foreclosure offer that requires
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment (i.e., forbearance, repayment or Trial Period Plan)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower communicates an intent to accept the alternative to foreclosure within 14
elements.label: text

elements.text: days from the date of the Borrower Evaluation Notice offer as described above, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must suspend the foreclosure sale where legally permitted under State or local
elements.label: text

elements.text: law. Additionally, in cases where a payment is required under the terms of an alternative
elements.label: text

elements.text: to foreclosure offer, and the Borrower communicates a written intent to accept an
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure offer, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Suspend the foreclosure sale until the last day of the month that the first payment is
elements.label: text

elements.text: due under the terms of the alternative to foreclosure offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delay the next legal action in the foreclosure process until the first month following
elements.label: text

elements.text: the end of the Trial Period Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delay the next legal action unless the Borrower breaches the plan, if the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: receives the first payment timely in accordance with the terms of the terms of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan or forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cancel the foreclosure action as permitted by State or local law, If the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: complies with the terms of the Trial Period or other plan and the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: modified with a fully executed modification agreement or the Delinquency is cured
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Foreclosure sale postponements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are delegated the authority to approve foreclosure sale postponements. When
elements.label: text

elements.text: determining whether to postpone a foreclosure sale, the Servicer must comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Sections 9301.7(a)(i), (ii), (iii) and (iv).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of the reason for the foreclosure sale postponement, in the event a foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale occurs, the Servicer’s performance will be measured against Freddie Mac’s State
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure timelines (refer to Exhibit 83).
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Exhibit 57A Approved Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation and Postponement of fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: and title expenses, for foreclosure attorney fees associated with delayed caused by
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with Sections 9301.7(a)(i), (ii), (iii) and (iv). Additionally, with prior written
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval from Freddie Mac, the Servicer may be reimbursed for foreclosure attorney fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: that exceed the expense limits in Exhibit 57A. To obtain written approval from Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, the Servicer must submit a request for pre-approval (RPA) using the RPA
elements.label: text

elements.text: functionality in PAID (Payments Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Tools).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a foreclosure sale is postponed due to Servicer non-compliance with the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, the Servicer failing to provide the foreclosure certification,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer will not receive any credit or consideration of State foreclosure timeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fees and will also be subject to any loss, expenses or other damages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reviews and certifications prior to foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Pre-sale account review by the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must have written policies and procedures requiring a review of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage at least 30 days prior to the scheduled foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must review the account history to verify compliance with all required
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower outreach, solicitation and evaluation requirements specified in Chapters 9101
elements.label: text

elements.text: and 9102 and that there is no approved payment arrangement, pending alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure offer or appeal for which the Borrower response period has not expired. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must document the results of their review in its Mortgage file or Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Certification to foreclosure counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least seven, but no more than 15 days prior to foreclosure sale, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: review the account and send written certification to the foreclosure counsel indicating that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure sale must continue unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The account review reveals that all Borrower outreach, solicitation and evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements have not been achieved, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is an approved payment arrangement, pending alternative to foreclosure offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: or appeal for which the Borrower response period has not expired
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any of the above exceptions apply, then the Servicer must not provide the certification
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must make every effort to stop a scheduled foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must document the results of its review in its Mortgage file or Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must work with the foreclosure counsel to develop a process for receipt of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure certification to prevent unnecessary delays. If the foreclosure counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: cancels the foreclosure sale due to the Servicer’s failure to provide the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification timely, the Servicer will be subject to compensatory fees for delays resulting
elements.label: text

elements.text: from such a cancelation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Foreclosure sale date extensions
elements.label: text

elements.text: In some instances, it may be in Freddie Mac’s best interest to extend the foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, cases when an extension of the sale will do one of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expedite the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expeditiously resolve litigation and/or bankruptcy in Freddie Mac’s favor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain a Borrower’s consent to the final judgment of foreclosure on an expedited basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that extending the foreclosure sale date is in Freddie Mac’s best
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, then the Servicer may use its discretion without obtaining Freddie Mac’s prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval to extend the sale. The Servicer must record the decision to extend the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale date and the basis for its decision in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure timeline requirements set forth in Section 9301.11(a) will not be waived in
elements.label: text

elements.text: consideration of extending the foreclosure sale date. Servicers and their counsel must use
elements.label: text

elements.text: their discretion to determine whether extending the foreclosure sale date is in Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: best interest based on the jurisdiction, the Mortgage and the Mortgaged Premises that is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject of the foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.8: Foreclosure sale and bidding requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delegated bidding
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to obtain a credit bid for foreclosure sale bidding
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements for and reimbursement of appraisal reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bidding instructions for First Lien Mortgages not covered by mortgage insurance or subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: to credit enhancements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bidding instructions for Mortgages covered by mortgage insurance or subject to credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: enhancements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bidding instructions for Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA or RHS
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bidding appropriately and pursuit of surplus proceeds or overbid funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Delegated bidding
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must offer a bid on all foreclosures. Freddie Mac delegates to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibility to establish the bid according to the guidelines in Sections 9301.8(d) through
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.8(f). However, if any of the following conditions exist, the Servicer must contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac at least ten Business Days prior to the foreclosure sale date:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Conditions that require the Servicer to contact Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condition Contact requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. It is not in Freddie Mac’s best
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest for the Servicer to enter a bid
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with the guidelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must e-mail Form 105,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Multipurpose Loan Servicing Transmittal,
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a First-Lien Mortgage to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: at ShortSales@FreddieMac.com
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. A hazard insurance claim is pending
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the bid will jeopardize the
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval or payout of the claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must e-mail Form 105 for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: First-Lien Mortgage to Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: Distressed_Property@FreddieMac.com
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Servicer is unable to obtain a
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit bid (see Section 9301.8(b) on
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtaining a credit bid for foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale bidding) and bid in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Section 9301.8(d), or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer disputes the credit bid
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained in connection with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: scheduled foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must e-mail Form 105 for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: First-Lien Mortgage to Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: ShortSales@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer disputes the credit bid
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained in connection with a scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale, and Freddie Mac decides
elements.label: text

elements.text: to update or change the credit bid obtained
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Servicer via the Freddie Mac Real
elements.label: text

elements.text: Estate Valuation and Pricing tool (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools), Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will notify the Servicer that the credit bid
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been updated prior to the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale date. (See Section 9301.8(b) on
elements.label: text

elements.text: updated credit bids.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Servicer is unable to obtain a
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit bid via the Freddie Mac Real
elements.label: text

elements.text: Estate Valuation and Pricing tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: (and bid in accordance with Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must e-mail Form 105 for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: First-Lien Mortgage to Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: ShortSales@FreddieMac.com
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.8(d)) because the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale is of a property subject to resale
elements.label: text

elements.text: restrictions (in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapters 4406, 4504 or 4502, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable), but the resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: terminate upon foreclosure (or
elements.label: text

elements.text: expiration of any applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: redemption period) or recordation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac provides bidding instructions and/or offers a bid for a foreclosure sale in
elements.label: text

elements.text: response to the contact requirements above, the Servicer must cooperate with Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: and make every effort to ensure that foreclosure counsel receives the updated bidding
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions in a timely manner so as not to delay, cancel or stop a scheduled foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) When to obtain a credit bid for foreclosure sale bidding
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain a credit bid when preparing bids on First Lien Mortgages not
elements.label: text

elements.text: covered by mortgage insurance, or when State law does not require that an appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: be used to set the bid (see Section 9202.4(a) on obtaining an appraisal report to prepare
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale bidding instructions). Servicers are not required to obtain credit bids when
elements.label: text

elements.text: preparing bids on properties subject to resale restrictions that survive foreclosure or
elements.label: text

elements.text: recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure (in accordance with Chapters 4406, 4502 or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4504, as applicable). The Servicer must refer to the applicable resale restrictions for details
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the property valuation and/or the restricted resale price, as well as any other terms and
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions that must be met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To ensure the Servicer has a credit bid in time for the foreclosure sale, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommends that the Servicer obtain the credit bid via the Freddie Mac Real Estate Valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Pricing tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) no less than 30 and no more than 90 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the scheduled foreclosure sale date (see Chapter 2404 for information about access to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Real Estate Valuation and Pricing tool). If the “good through date” returned with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit bid is set to expire prior to the scheduled foreclosure sale date, then the Servicer needs
elements.label: text

elements.text: to proactively request an updated credit bid in time for the scheduled foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may decide, in its sole discretion or in response to a Servicer’s dispute of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit bid (see Section 9301.8(a) regarding a Servicer’s dispute of the credit bid obtained in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with a scheduled foreclosure sale), to update or change the credit bid obtained by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer via the Real Estate Valuation and Pricing tool. In such cases, Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: notify the Servicer that the credit bid has been updated prior to the foreclosure sale date, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must cooperate with Freddie Mac and make every effort to ensure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure counsel receives the updated bidding instructions in a timely manner so as not to
elements.label: text

elements.text: delay, cancel or stop a scheduled foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is unable to provide the updated bidding instructions to foreclosure counsel in
elements.label: text

elements.text: a timely manner, or foreclosure counsel could not use the updated bidding instructions, then
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must document it in the Mortgage file. Under no circumstance should the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale be delayed because of the Servicer’s receipt of an updated credit bid.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Requirements for and reimbursement of appraisal reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Requirements for appraisals
elements.label: text

elements.text: If State or local law requires an appraisal report to establish the bid amount, and Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac has given the Servicer approval to obtain an appraisal report pursuant to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions of Section 9202.4(a), then the appraisal report must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Be completed in accordance with the requirements in Topic 5600
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Be dated no more than 120 days before the foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Be completed by an appraiser who meets the requirements of Section 5603.1 and who
elements.label: text

elements.text: is free from any conflict of interest or financial interest in the transaction other than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser’s reasonable fee for such an opinion
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Be based on an exterior inspection, if an interior inspection cannot be obtained
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Document any conditions that relate to the existence of Hazardous Substances or
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions that would affect the habitability, safety, value or occupancy of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the appraisal report has become greater than 120 days old, the Servicer must have
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appraiser recertify the appraisal report. The Servicer must not order a new appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Reimbursement of appraisal reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for the cost of an appraisal report ordered in
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with Section 9202.4(a)(i) and this section. If the Servicer needs to reaffirm
elements.label: text

elements.text: the value of the property after 120 days, Freddie Mac will reimburse for a recertification
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Bidding instructions for First Lien Mortgages not covered by mortgage insurance or
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to credit enhancements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a First-Lien Mortgage not covered by mortgage insurance or subject to a credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: enhancement, the Servicer must bid in accordance with the requirements provided below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Unless State law requires that an appraisal report be used to set the bid or the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale is of a property subject to resale restrictions that survive foreclosure or recordation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure (in accordance with Chapters 4406, 4502 or 4504, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable), Freddie Mac recommends that the Servicer obtain the credit bid via the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Real Estate Valuation and Pricing tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) no
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than 30 days, but no greater than 90 days prior to the scheduled foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: (See Section 9301.8(b) on obtaining a credit bid for foreclosure sale bidding and Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9202.4(a) on obtaining an appraisal report to prepare foreclosure sale bidding
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Servicer must then start its bid at the minimum required by State law. If State law
elements.label: text

elements.text: would impose a redemption, confirmation or ratification period because the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale price was below a certain amount, the Servicer must bid a sufficient amount that
elements.label: text

elements.text: would minimize or avoid any such additional periods from being imposed. Also, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable State law gives the Borrower a post-foreclosure sale right of redemption, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower can redeem the property for the successful foreclosure sale price, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must start its bid, as applicable, at an amount equal to the lesser of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 100% of the credit bid obtained from the Real Estate Valuation and Pricing tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Total indebtedness, which includes the UPB, accrued interest, Escrow advances and
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses (see Exhibit 57A, Approved Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Postponement Fees and Title Expenses, for the applicable expense limits)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Such other amount as may be required by applicable State law (e.g., amount of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: judgment); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For the foreclosure sale of a property subject to resale restrictions that survive
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the property valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or the restricted resale price
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. In the event the Servicer worked with its foreclosure attorney to preserve either Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s or the MI’s right to pursue a deficiency action pursuant to Sections 9301.6(a)(ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and 9301.8(e), respectively, or filed a judicial foreclosure to preserve the right to pursue a
elements.label: text

elements.text: deficiency judgment pursuant to Section 9301.3(a), the Servicer should bid an amount at
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale that reserves the right to pursue deficiencies after the foreclosure sale on
elements.label: text

elements.text: a case-by-case basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. If a third party enters a bid, the Servicer must bid, as applicable, an amount up to, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceeding, an amount equal to the lesser of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 100% of the credit bid obtained from the Real Estate Valuation and Pricing tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Total indebtedness, which includes the UPB, accrued interest, Escrow advances and
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses (see Exhibit 57A for the applicable expense limits)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Such other amount as may be required by applicable State law (e.g., amount of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: judgment); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For the foreclosure sale of a property subject to resale restrictions that survive
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the property valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or the restricted resale price
elements.label: text

elements.text: As applicable, in the event that the minimum bid required by State law exceeds the credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: bid but is less than total indebtedness or such other amount as may be required by State
elements.label: text

elements.text: law, then the Servicer does not need to bid an amount more than the minimum bid
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by State law. However, if the minimum bid required by State law exceeds the
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit bid, total indebtedness, and such other amount as may be required by State law, or,
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the foreclosure sale of a property subject to resale restrictions that survive foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, exceeds the property valuation and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: restricted resale price, then the Servicer must follow the instructions in Section 9301.8(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Notwithstanding the requirements above, for the foreclosure sale of a property subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: with resale restrictions that survive foreclosure or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, the Servicer must refer to the applicable resale restrictions for details on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property valuation and/or the restricted resale price, as well as any other terms and
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions that must be met
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Bidding instructions for Mortgages covered by mortgage insurance or subject to credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: enhancements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a First Lien Mortgage covered by mortgage insurance, the Servicer must bid an amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by the MI. If the MI elects not to issue bidding instructions, or Freddie Mac has a
elements.label: text

elements.text: delegation of authority from the MI for foreclosure sale bidding, the Servicer must follow the
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions in Section 9301.8(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding the above, in the event the Mortgage has mortgage insurance and the MI’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: bidding instructions or requirements include preserving deficiency rights, where permitted by
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law, regardless if the MI has delegated authority to Freddie Mac for foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale bidding, the Servicer must preserve the MI’s right to pursue a deficiency action and
elements.label: text

elements.text: should bid an amount at foreclosure sale that reserves the right to pursue deficiencies after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure sale on a case-by-case basis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Mortgage subject to a credit enhancement, the Servicer must bid an amount approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the responsible party.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Bidding instructions for Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA or
elements.label: text

elements.text: RHS
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA or RHS, then the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must follow FHA, VA or RHS guidelines for bidding instructions. If the Mortgage is a VA
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, then the Servicer may not enter a bid on Freddie Mac’s behalf in excess of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: upset price established by the VA. If the Servicer does not receive the VA upset price, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must contact Freddie Mac for instructions by submitting Form 105, Multipurpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Servicing Transmittal, to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Bidding appropriately and pursuit of surplus proceeds or overbid funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must bid at the foreclosure sale:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In accordance with the requirements in Sections 9301.8(a) through 9301.8(f), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ To preserve Freddie Mac’s rights to a deficiency according to the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 9301.6(a)(ii) through 9301.6(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the property is purchased by a third party at the foreclosure sale and there are any surplus
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds or overbid funds, the Servicer must pursue and claim to retain the funds, so long as
elements.label: text

elements.text: it is determined to be in Freddie Mac’s best interest, to the extent:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The funds will satisfy or reduce the total indebtedness under the terms of the Mortgage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: such other amount as may be allowed by State law (e.g., amount and terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: judgment) and the costs and expenses of the sale, if possible; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ It is permitted under applicable State law
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer knows that additional attorney fees will be incurred when pursuing any
elements.label: text

elements.text: surplus proceeds or overbid funds, and the Servicer believes it is in Freddie Mac’s best
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest to pursue and claim to retain any surplus proceeds or overbid funds, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must submit a request for pre-approval (RPA) to Freddie Mac via PAID (Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) to recommend
elements.label: text

elements.text: incurring additional attorney fees.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.9 Reporting foreclosure sale results, managing
elements.label: text

elements.text: remittance requirements for redemptions and
elements.label: text

elements.text: confirmation date changes and third-party sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds and expense reimbursements (10/08/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting foreclosure sale results
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting and remittance requirements for redemptions and confirmation date changes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Third-party sale proceeds, reimbursement of expenses and reporting and remittance
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ File retention for foreclosure proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Reporting foreclosure sale results
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must use the Resolve® user interface (UI) to report the results of a foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac. Servicers should refer to Resolve Online Help for details on the process and
elements.label: text

elements.text: best practices to report the results of foreclosure sales. Servicers and any Users that use or
elements.label: text

elements.text: access Resolve are bound by all of the provisions of the Master System License (see Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2401.1) and the System-Specific License for Servicing Tools (see Section 2404.2). Each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer shall use Resolve in accordance with (A) the requirements in this section, (B) the
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions provided in Resolve Online Help and any other documentation and (C) any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other applicable provisions of the Guide, including Sections 2401.1 and 2404.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of the results of all foreclosure sales, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO and successful third-party bids. To notify Freddie Mac of the results of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale, the Servicer must complete the applicable data fields in the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale reporting template, including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The sale result
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Total debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actual credit bid amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property occupancy status type
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Attorney name and phone number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer point of contact and phone number; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether deficiency rights were preserved
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must upload the foreclosure sale reporting template in Resolve no later than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day immediately following the date of the foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the result of a foreclosure sale reported to Freddie Mac needs to be corrected or
elements.label: text

elements.text: removed for reasons noted in Section 9301.10(a)(i), the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: by requesting a “rollback” pursuant to Section 9301.10.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once a foreclosure sale is held, a Servicer does not need to continue to report applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: information through EDR for the Mortgage. (See Section 9102.6 regarding monthly EDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: transmissions.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Compensatory fee for failing to comply with reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Failure to comply with the requirements of Section 9301.9(a) will result in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment of a compensatory fee to the Servicer of $100 per day for every day that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale is not reported correctly. If the results of the foreclosure sale are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported correctly, Freddie Mac may exercise its remedies provided by the Guide and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: other Purchase Documents. Refer to Chapter 3602 regarding repurchases, repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternatives and other remedies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will bill the Servicer for such compensatory fees on the Servicer Non￾Performing Loans Invoice. Refer to Section 9102.1 for information on the payment of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Non-Performing Loans Invoices via an Automated Clearing House draft.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are not required to but may appeal a compensatory fee for late foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting. Appeals must be submitted to Freddie Mac via the Freddie Mac Default Fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appeal System.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac considers the notification to be correctly reported when Freddie Mac has
elements.label: text

elements.text: received and successfully processed the foreclosure sale/DIL submission without errors.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reporting and remittance requirements for redemptions and confirmation date
elements.label: text

elements.text: changes
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac if either the projected expiration of the redemption
elements.label: text

elements.text: period changes, or the scheduled confirmation date changes from the date the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: notified Freddie Mac of the foreclosure sale/deed-in-lieu of foreclosure via Resolve®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report the change by submitting Form 105, Multipurpose Loan Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transmittal, to Freddie Mac (see Directory 6) no later than one Business Day after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer receives notification of the change.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Redemptions of REO properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting requirements for REO redemptions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac that the REO property is redeemed no later
elements.label: text

elements.text: than one Business Day after redemption by completing and submitting Form 105 to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (see Directory 6) for a 1-unit property or 2- to 4-unit properties. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac must execute any documents related to the redemption, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must send the documents with Form 105 to the appropriate address.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remitting REO redemption proceeds – 1-unit and 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the property is redeemed, the Servicer must forward to Freddie Mac the principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: and interest amount due (through the redemption date), as well as all amounts that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursable by Freddie Mac pursuant to Chapter 9701 within five Business Days of
elements.label: text

elements.text: receiving the redemption proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If included in the redemption proceeds, the Servicer may net out any amounts that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: due to the Servicer from the Borrower but are not reimbursable by Freddie Mac (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: late fees, NSF fees, property inspections and other items permitted by applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: law); that is, so long as the redemption proceeds after any netting are sufficient to
elements.label: text

elements.text: cover the amounts required to be forwarded to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the redemption proceeds are not sufficient to cover the amounts required to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: forwarded to Freddie Mac, then the Servicer must remit the entire redemption
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Checks should be made payable to the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: must reference the property address and the nine-digit Freddie Mac loan number on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the check. Remittances should be forwarded to the appropriate business address
elements.label: text

elements.text: below.

elements.label: text

elements.text: and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88) System available at https://sf.freddiemac.com/working￾with-us/servicing/resources.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Reporting and remittance requirements for redemptions following third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: The remittance requirements for redemptions following third-party foreclosure sales are
elements.label: text

elements.text: the same as the remittance requirements for third-party foreclosure sales. If a Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: receives redemption proceeds, the Servicer must remit the required principal and interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: owed to Freddie Mac (as required in Section 8303.3(b)) no later than five Business Days
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the Servicer receives the proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must comply with the investor reporting and remitting requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8303.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, after remitting the required amount to Freddie Mac, the Servicer is owed reimbursable
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses or reimbursable principal and interest, then the Servicer must request
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement of expenses following the redemption in the same manner it requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement following a third-party foreclosure sale, as described in Section 9301.9(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Third-party sale proceeds, reimbursement of expenses and reporting and remittance
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must use the Resolve user interface (UI) to report the results of a third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale to Freddie Mac and settle the transaction. Servicers should refer to Resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: Online Help for details on the process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Remitting the sale proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a third party purchases the property at the foreclosure sale the Servicer must instruct
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure counsel conducting the sale to remit the sale proceeds (i.e., sale proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: without any netting by foreclosure counsel of their fees and/or costs from the sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds check) to the Servicer via overnight mail or wire transfer no later than three
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Days after the foreclosure counsel receives the proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer has not received the sale proceeds from the foreclosure counsel who
elements.label: text

elements.text: conducted the sale within 60 days from the foreclosure sale, then the Servicer should
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide a status update to Freddie Mac at ShortSales@FreddieMac.com, and on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly basis thereafter until the Servicer receives the proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Obtaining reimbursement for foreclosure counsel fees and costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: The foreclosure counsel must obtain reimbursement of all fees and costs from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer. The foreclosure counsel should not net their fees and/or costs from the sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds check. If applicable law mandates that certain expenses (e.g., sheriff or
elements.label: text

elements.text: auctioneer fees) be deducted from the sale proceeds before remitting them to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure counsel, the Servicer must instruct the foreclosure counsel to send the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer an itemization of the distribution of the proceeds with the sale proceeds. This
elements.label: text

elements.text: itemization of the distribution of the proceeds may be needed as supporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation if the Servicer intends to be reimbursed for any shortage in the sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Freddie Mac will not reimburse the Servicer for foreclosure fees and costs incurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: on a Mortgage that was sold to Freddie Mac with recourse.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Reporting and remittance for third-party sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: To complete the third-party foreclosure sale, the Servicer must complete the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: steps:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of the third-party sale no later than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day immediately following the date of the foreclosure sale pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9301.9(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Following the Servicer’s receipt of the entire amount of the proceeds from a third￾party sale, the Servicer must complete all reporting and remittance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to Section 8303.3(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. After the Servicer completes all reporting and remittance requirements pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8303.3(b), Resolve will automatically settle the third-party sale with no
elements.label: text

elements.text: further action required by the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. If third-party sale proceeds equal or exceed total indebtedness (e.g., the UPB, accrued
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, Escrow advances and expenses), Servicers may (but are not required to)
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit expenses in PAID. However, Freddie Mac will not reimburse Servicers for
elements.label: text

elements.text: these expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. If third-party sale proceeds do not exceed total indebtedness, then Servicers must
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit all expenses related to the third-party sale via PAID (see Exhibit 88, Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tools) Refer to Section 9701.1(e) for time frames to submit expenses. The time frame
elements.label: text

elements.text: to submit expenses for a third-party sale begins once the transaction has been settled
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Resolve.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When submitting reimbursement requests, Servicers must offset these expenses or
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts by income as set forth in Section 9701.4(d). Additionally, for expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement requests on Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA
elements.label: text

elements.text: or RHS not subject to recourse or indemnification, reimbursement will occur after
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac receives the claim payment from the applicable entity and determines
elements.label: text

elements.text: that all other requirements for reimbursement have been satisfied. Refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9603.2(a) for FHA/VA/RHS claim filing and Section 9603.2(d) for receipt of claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Based on the data entered in the foreclosure sale reporting template and uploaded in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolve, once the third-party sale transaction is settled, if the Servicer is owed
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement of any shortage in the sale proceeds and/or reimbursable expenses or
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts, including amounts not legally collectable from the third-party purchaser,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then PAID will automatically reimburse the Servicer based on the expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order for the Servicer to be reimbursed, Freddie Mac must receive, accept and
elements.label: text

elements.text: settle the third-party foreclosure sale transaction submitted via Resolve. Additionally,
elements.label: text

elements.text: failure to submit the third-party foreclosure sale transaction in the appropriate time
elements.label: text

elements.text: frame could forfeit the Servicer’s ability to be reimbursed, in part or in whole, for
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise reimbursable expenses or amounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers and any Users that use or access Resolve are bound by all of the provisions of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Master System License (see Section 2401.1) and the System-Specific License for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Tools (see Section 2404.2), as specifically modified by this paragraph.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Resolve Online Help for information on processing third-party foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: transactions through Resolve.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9204.2(a) for additional requirements when submitting a workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: or transaction via Resolve for approval and closing in Freddie Mac’s systems.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Supporting documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac generally will not require the Servicer to send supporting documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the submission of a third-party foreclosure sale transaction via Resolve. However,
elements.label: text

elements.text: there are situations where Freddie Mac may instruct the Servicer to provide standard
elements.label: text

elements.text: supporting documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standard supporting documentation includes, but is not limited to, the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 1160, Third-Party Sale Transmittal Worksheet, with Section D Indebtedness
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed, itemizing the complete total debt figure including the UPB, accrued
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, Escrow advances less any positive Escrow balances, as well as expenses after
elements.label: text

elements.text: being offset by any income (pursuant to Section 9701.4(d))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Itemization of the distribution of the sale proceeds, if the sale proceeds are less than
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sale price (e.g., if the foreclosure court order required the payment of sheriff
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses from the sale price). If the sale proceeds equal 100% of the sale price, then
elements.label: text

elements.text: this itemization is not needed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copy of the BPO or appraisal report, if used for the foreclosure bid
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copy of the sale proceeds check, or other documentation (e.g., proof of wire transfer)
elements.label: text

elements.text: verifying the amount of sale proceeds received
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copy of the sheriff’s appraisal, or other documentation used to establish the opening
elements.label: text

elements.text: bid, if required by applicable State law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If there are amounts not legally collectable from the third-party purchaser that need to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be reimbursed to the Servicer, supporting documentation, including but not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable State statute
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will contact the Servicer should it require supporting documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: following submission of a third-party foreclosure sale transaction via Resolve.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) File retention for foreclosure proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain accurate and complete records of the foreclosure proceedings for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages in the Mortgage file. The Servicer must maintain the Mortgage file for at least
elements.label: text

elements.text: seven years from the date of the foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.10: Post-foreclosure requirements and title
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: considerations (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rollbacks
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery of clear and marketable title
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Vesting the title and avoiding transfer taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Rollbacks
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the result of a foreclosure sale reported to Freddie Mac needs to be corrected or removed
elements.label: text

elements.text: for various reasons including, but not limited to, the reasons noted in subsection (i), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must notify Freddie Mac by requesting a “rollback.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac, via Freddie Mac Servicing Data Corrections
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools), within one Business Day of the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination, in coordination with foreclosure counsel, that the foreclosure sale is
elements.label: text

elements.text: legally invalid or void (e.g., a prior bankruptcy filing renders the sale void).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Following the Servicer’s determination that the foreclosure sale is legally invalid
elements.label: text

elements.text: or void, if appropriate, the Servicer must take legal action to obtain relief and seek
elements.label: text

elements.text: validation of the foreclosure sale. The Servicer must process the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: according to all applicable Guide requirements and continue to monitor the status
elements.label: text

elements.text: of all pending legal action(s). For example, if a bankruptcy petition has been filed
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Servicer files a motion to obtain relief or annul the automatic stay and
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain validation of the foreclosure sale, the Servicer must monitor the status of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the motion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subsequent to Freddie Mac’s receipt and processing of the rollback, and prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: any validation of the foreclosure sale, if any, the Servicer must continue to report
elements.label: text

elements.text: all applicable information through EDR for the Mortgage. (See Section 9102.6
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding monthly EDR transmissions.) If the court grants a motion for relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or enters an order validating the foreclosure sale, the Servicer must re-report
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure sale results (pursuant to the reporting requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.9(a)) within one Business Day of entry of the order by the court.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In circumstances where a Chapter 11, 12 or 13 bankruptcy filing would not
elements.label: text

elements.text: legally invalidate or void the foreclosure sale but the Servicer is applying
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments under any bankruptcy repayment plan, the Servicer must notify Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, via the Servicing Data Corrections tool. In these cases, it may be in Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s best interest to process a rollback to allow the Servicer to report applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: information relating to the bankruptcy repayment plan. Subsequent to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s receipt and processing of the rollback, if the court dismisses the bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: case or grants a motion for relief from the automatic stay, the Servicer must re￾report the foreclosure sale results within one Business Day of entry of the order
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the court and use the date of entry of the order as the foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. If a third party enters an upset bid per applicable State law on a property that Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac acquired at the foreclosure sale (i.e., REO property), the Servicer must notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, via the Servicing Data Corrections tool, within one Business Day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: upset bid.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report the foreclosure sale results based on the final upset bid
elements.label: text

elements.text: within one Business Day of receiving notification from Freddie Mac that the rollback
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been processed, and the upset bid period has expired.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. If the property is sold to a third party, but the sale falls through and the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines it must foreclose the Mortgaged Premises again, the Servicer must notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac of its determination within one Business Day via the Servicing Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corrections tool.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event the Mortgaged Premises needs to be re-foreclosed, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: process the foreclosure according to all applicable Guide requirements once the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure action is validated. Additionally, subsequent to Freddie Mac’s receipt and
elements.label: text

elements.text: processing of the rollback, the Servicer must continue to report all applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: information through EDR for the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. If the Servicer incorrectly reported that Freddie Mac acquired the property at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale and needs to re-report the correct foreclosure sale results, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via the Servicing Data Corrections tool.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report the correct foreclosure sale results within one Business Day
elements.label: text

elements.text: of receiving notification from Freddie Mac that the rollback has been processed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. If the Servicer approved the Borrower for an alternative to foreclosure prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale, but did not cancel the foreclosure sale, the Servicer must notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac within one Business Day, via the Servicing Data Corrections tool, its
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination that the foreclosure sale can be rescinded
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must contact the foreclosure counsel to confirm the foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: can be rescinded and indicate such determination in the Servicing Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corrections tool when notifying Freddie Mac of the rollback
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must comply with the requirements set forth in the Guide and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents for the applicable alternative to foreclosure. To the extent
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted by applicable law, the Servicer must not rescind the foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: until the Borrower executes the applicable agreement, makes the appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment (if required) and otherwise complies with the terms of the alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subsequent to Freddie Mac’s receipt and processing of the rollback, if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower does not comply with the requirements of the applicable alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, the Servicer must cancel the agreement and notify Freddie Mac via
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicing Data Corrections tool within one Business Day of the cancelation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Servicer must comply with all other reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable to the specific alternative to foreclosure, and re-report the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale results immediately following all other reporting requirements being
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Post-sale alternative to foreclosure reviews and/or approvals are generally
elements.label: text

elements.text: prohibited. A rollback request due to the approval of an alternative to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the foreclosure sale will not be processed by Freddie Mac except for reasons
elements.label: text

elements.text: noted in this Section 9301.10(a)(i) or any other applicable Guide sections.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, if payments are made and applied by the Servicer after the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale, in absence of a pre-sale agreement, it may be in Freddie Mac’s best interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: to process a rollback.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all rollback requests, the Servicer must ensure that title vests to the appropriate party
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the event the rollback has been processed, and/or the foreclosure sale has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: rescinded.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer does not need to request a rollback if the Servicer reported that a third party
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchased the property at the foreclosure sale, erroneously or otherwise, and needs to
elements.label: text

elements.text: report that Freddie Mac acquired the property at the foreclosure sale. Instead, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must re-report the foreclosure sale results within one Business Day of the Servicer’s
elements.label: text
