elements.text: determination that Freddie Mac acquired the property at the foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Compensatory fee for failing to comply with reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer will be assessed a compensatory fee in an amount equal to $1,000 per
elements.label: text

elements.text: occurrence for administrative costs plus any third-party costs when Freddie Mac must
elements.label: text

elements.text: process a rollback in accordance with Section 9301.10(a)(i). Freddie Mac will not
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimburse foreclosure fees and costs for the improper foreclosure that resulted in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: rollback.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will bill the Servicer for such compensatory fees on the Servicer Non￾Performing Loans Invoice. Refer to Section 9102.1 for information on the payment of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer Non-Performing Loans Invoices via an Automated Clearing House draft.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac considers the notification to be correctly reported when Freddie Mac has
elements.label: text

elements.text: received and successfully processed the foreclosure sale/deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission without errors.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Delivery of clear and marketable title
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Property located in a State without a redemption or confirmation period
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Servicer is the purchaser of the property at a foreclosure sale, it must ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the foreclosure counsel provides Freddie Mac with clear and marketable title to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property after the foreclosure sale. The title must be free of any liens, claims, defects and
elements.label: text

elements.text: encumbrances. The title must be marketable so Freddie Mac can sell the property freely
elements.label: text

elements.text: to others. Issuance or purchase of a letter of indemnity, title insurance or similar form of
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnification does not constitute, and may not be used in lieu of, provision of clear and
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketable title to the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must instruct the foreclosure counsel to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Submit the foreclosure deed for recordation within one Business Day after receipt of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Obtain the recorder’s receipt as evidence that the deed was presented for recordation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Send the Servicer the recorder’s receipt within three Business Days after receiving it
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the recorder
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Provide the recorded deed to the Servicer within three Business Days after receiving
elements.label: text

elements.text: the deed from the recorder’s office. The Servicer must retain the deed in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Property located in a State with a redemption or confirmation period
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the redemption period has expired or the foreclosure sale has been confirmed, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must ensure that clear and marketable title is obtained as stated in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Executing documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac needs to execute a document for the Servicer to process the foreclosure,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or execute a document related to a foreclosure sale, the Servicer must submit Form 105,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Multipurpose Loan Servicing Transmittal, to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: supporting documentation, which may include, but is not limited to, the last recorded
elements.label: text

elements.text: document in the chain of title, and include the document Freddie Mac needs to execute.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Vesting the title and avoiding transfer taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the foreclosure sale, or when closing a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure transaction, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must ensure that title to the property is vested to the appropriate party. (See Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9209.6 regarding closing, reporting and remittance requirements for a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure transaction.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Conventional Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the foreclosure sale (if the property is not purchased by a third party) or when
elements.label: text

elements.text: closing a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure transaction, the Servicer must ensure that title to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property is vested in Freddie Mac’s name; that is, unless it is in Freddie Mac’s best
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest to have the title to the property vested in the Servicer’s name after the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale. In such cases, the Servicer must then have the title to the property transferred to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac via special warranty deed or quitclaim deed and must ensure that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer of title will not impair Freddie Mac’s sale of the REO.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In Florida, a special warranty deed is required to transfer the title to the property to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac. If applicable, the transfer to Freddie Mac’s name should generally be
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed within 30 days of vesting the title in the Servicer’s name.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If having the title to the property vested in the Servicer’s name after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale limits the liability for Freddie Mac to reimburse homeowners association
elements.label: text

elements.text: (HOA) assessments, then the Servicer should proceed in such a manner. If the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines it is in Freddie Mac’s best interest, the Servicer must record in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file the decision to vest the title in the Servicer’s name and the basis for its decision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the foreclosure sale, or when closing a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure transaction, title
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the property should be vested in a manner that does not result in an obligation to pay
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer taxes. Freddie Mac will not reimburse the Servicer for any transfer taxes, unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Local authorities require the Servicer to pay the transfer tax in order to record a deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: and ensure that title vests appropriately
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The transfer tax is paid under protest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer submits the request for written pre-approval (RPA) for reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the transfer tax via PAID (Payments Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) (see Section 9701.4(a)), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Counsel could not process the foreclosure and/or the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction in a manner that would successfully avoid the imposition of the transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: tax obligation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers will not be reimbursed for transfer taxes if any of the above conditions and
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements do not exist or are not met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the foreclosure involves a Manufactured Home in a certificate of title State, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must conduct the replevin or other legal action necessary to repossess the home
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Servicer’s name and have the new certificate of title issued in Freddie Mac’s name.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA or RHS
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must follow FHA, VA or RHS guidelines for conveying title to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosed property to the applicable agency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.11: State foreclosure timelines and performance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ State foreclosure timelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Allowable delays in completing a foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ State foreclosure timeline performance assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) State foreclosure timelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: A foreclosure timeline is the time it takes to process a foreclosure. The timeline is measured
elements.label: text

elements.text: in days from the DDLPI to the date of the foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Therefore, the timeline consists of the time it should take from the DDLPI to the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is referred to foreclosure counsel (see Sections 9301.2(a) through 9301.2(d)), plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: the time it takes from the referral date of foreclosure to the foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The timeline does not include any post-sale redemption or confirmation periods. Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: has a timeline for each State which is the number of days it should take to process a
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure in the State under most circumstances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For conventional Mortgages, the Servicer must complete the foreclosure sale within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure timeline (from DDLPI to foreclosure sale) for the State in which the property is
elements.label: text

elements.text: located, as listed in Exhibit 83, Freddie Mac State Foreclosure Timelines. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with FHA, VA and RHS timelines for all Mortgages insured by the FHA or
elements.label: text

elements.text: guaranteed by the VA or RHS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the foreclosure sale was delayed due to one of the allowable State foreclosure timeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: delays listed in Section 9301.11(b), the timeline from DDLPI to the foreclosure sale date will
elements.label: text

elements.text: be increased to account for the allowable delay based on the information the Servicer reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac via EDR. Refer to Exhibit 83A, Determining State Foreclosure Timeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: Compensatory Fees, for details on how Freddie Mac determines the additional time to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: granted for allowable delays.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must have procedures and processes in place to manage its State foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: timeline performance. To assist with monitoring performance, the Servicer may access
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports, accessible via the “Foreclosures” tile of the Servicer’s Servicer Performance Profile
elements.label: text

elements.text: (SPP) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools), on foreclosure sales completed. The reports in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: SPP will be based on information and data the Servicer reported to Freddie Mac. Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: should review the reports regularly to ensure the information and data they reported was
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete and accurate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reduction in timelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: At any time and in its sole discretion, Freddie Mac may reduce a State’s foreclosure timeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: set forth in Exhibit 83 and further, may subject loans to revised timelines and associated
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee calculations that were referred to foreclosure as of the effective date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reduced timeline.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Allowable delays in completing a foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 83, Freddie Mac State Foreclosure Timelines, sets forth Freddie Mac’s foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: timeline for each State. Freddie Mac’s State foreclosure timeline (DDLPI to foreclosure sale)
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be extended for a Mortgage under the following circumstances, provided the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: complies with the applicable EDR requirements (refer to Section 9102.6 for information on
elements.label: text

elements.text: EDR reporting requirements):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When a Borrower files for bankruptcy protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delays due to probate, military indulgence and contested foreclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delays caused by the Borrower being offered or entering into a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification Trial Period plan but failing to comply with the terms of the plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delays caused by the Borrower entering into an unemployment forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delays caused by the Borrower exercising his or her right to appeal a modification denial,
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to Section 9101.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Exhibit 83A, Determining State Foreclosure Timeline Performance Compensatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fees, for information on how Freddie Mac calculates the additional time granted for each of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the allowable delays listed above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Moreover, a Servicer may delay completing the foreclosure, if necessary, when the delay is
elements.label: text

elements.text: required due to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Applicable federal, State or local law, but only if the delay was necessary or inevitable
elements.label: text

elements.text: despite the Servicer’s best efforts to incorporate such laws into its foreclosure procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: and timelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Emergency, exigent or unusual circumstances that do not occur in the regular course of
elements.label: text

elements.text: business and that are both unforeseeable and outside the control of the Servicer (this
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes delays resulting from Eligible Disasters, pursuant to Section 8404.1(d))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer waiting for instructions from Freddie Mac on how to proceed with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: distressed property (see Section 8403.1(a) and (b) for an explanation of a distressed
elements.label: text

elements.text: property)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer being unable to complete the foreclosure sale due to the Borrower being
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved for, or performing under, the terms of an alternative to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s approval for a Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale or Freddie Mac Standard
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure being based on a review of a First Complete Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package, pursuant to Section 9101.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower being conditionally approved for mortgage assistance under the State
elements.label: text

elements.text: Housing Finance Agency program in accordance with Section 9211.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of a delay for any of the above reasons, the Servicer’s State foreclosure timeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: performance will be measured against Freddie Mac’s State foreclosure timeline and
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fees. The Servicer may appeal the decision and must provide to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: at the time of appeal any and all information and documentation supporting the claim that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: delay was necessary and required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) State foreclosure timeline performance assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages that resulted in a foreclosure sale on or after January 1, 2019:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Determination of State foreclosure timeline performance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will evaluate the Servicer’s State foreclosure timeline performance on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: calendar year basis. Based on all foreclosures the Servicer completes in the year being
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluated, Freddie Mac will determine a Servicer’s State foreclosure timeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: performance for each Mortgage that went to foreclosure sale in the year being evaluated
elements.label: text

elements.text: on a national basis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will determine how long it took the Servicer to complete each foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale based on the information that the Servicer reports to Freddie Mac via the monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: EDR submissions and the foreclosure sale transmission after the completion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale. Freddie Mac will calculate the amount of compensatory fees, if any, in
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition to any actual losses, costs or damages caused by Servicer non-compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Guide, including State foreclosure timeline requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To determine the Servicer’s overall State foreclosure timeline performance, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will complete the following steps:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Step 1 – Calculating actual foreclosure timeline performance for each Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will determine how many days it took the Servicer to complete each
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale during the calendar year and whether the number of days exceeded or
elements.label: text

elements.text: were managed under the applicable State foreclosure timeline standard as listed in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 83, Freddie Mac State Foreclosure Timelines. Freddie Mac will then calculate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee amount for each individual foreclosure sale to determine whether each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage will result in a compensatory fee or credit. The amounts calculated in this step
elements.label: text

elements.text: on an individual foreclosure sale basis will be negative (a credit) for those foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales that were managed under that State’s foreclosure timeline and will be positive (a
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee) for those foreclosure sales that exceeded that State’s foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: timeline.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Step 2 – Calculating the aggregate compensatory fee on a national basis:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will calculate the Servicer’s aggregate State foreclosure timeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee amount by netting the compensatory fees and credits determined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Step 1 for all foreclosure sales that were completed in the calendar year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Step 3 – Determining whether to assess a compensatory fee:
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer’s aggregate compensatory fee calculated in Step 2 is $300,000 or less, then
elements.label: text

elements.text: no compensatory fee will be assessed. However, if the Servicer’s aggregate
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee calculated in Step 2 is greater than $300,000, and the Servicer is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise exempt under Section 9301.11(c)(ii), a compensatory fee will be assessed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Exhibit 83A, Determining State Foreclosure Timeline Performance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Compensatory Fees, for additional details regarding how Freddie Mac calculates
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fees.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The State foreclosure timelines used in determining the Servicer’s performance will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjusted for the period of time for which the foreclosures were delayed due to any of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: allowable delays in Section 9301.11(b), provided the Servicer complied with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable EDR requirement and/or the appeals process in Section 9301.11(c)(iii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Compensatory fee process
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer’s performance exceeded Freddie Mac’s State foreclosure timeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements as outlined in Section 9301.10(c)(i), then the compensatory fee process is as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Step 1: Freddie Mac will automatically waive a Servicer’s potential State foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: timeline compensatory fees if its exposure amount is $300,000 or less for that
elements.label: text

elements.text: calendar year. This is considered the de minimis billing exception. (Note: $300,000 =
elements.label: text

elements.text: previous monthly de minimis threshold of $25,000 x 12 months.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Step 2: For those Servicers that did not receive a waiver under Step 1, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will calculate the Servicer’s overall Servicer Success Scorecard ranking for that
elements.label: text

elements.text: calendar year against the respective rank group the Servicer belongs to on December
elements.label: text

elements.text: 31 of that calendar year (see Section 3501.2(b) regarding Servicer performance
elements.label: text

elements.text: results). If a Servicer’s overall ranking for that year is in the top 75% of its rank group
elements.label: text

elements.text: on December 31 (i.e., not in the bottom 25% of that rank group), its State foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: timeline compensatory fees will automatically be waived.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: This step does not apply to a Servicer where an overall ranking is not provided,
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether because no Servicers in that Servicer’s rank group are provided an overall
elements.label: text

elements.text: ranking or the individual Servicer did not receive an overall ranking within its rank
elements.label: text

elements.text: group. Additional information on rankings and the Servicer Success Scorecard is
elements.label: text

elements.text: available through Freddie Mac Gateway®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Step 3: If a Servicer remains subject to the assessment of State foreclosure timeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fees after steps 1 and 2, the Servicer may be placed into a performance
elements.label: text

elements.text: monitoring plan. If the Servicer is placed into a performance monitoring plan, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment of its compensatory fee exposure balance will be suspended until Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac determines whether the Servicer met the terms of the plan. If Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines that a Servicer complied with the required terms of the plan, its State
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure timeline compensatory fee assessment will be waived automatically. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer is not eligible for a performance monitoring plan, the Servicer will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessed its compensatory fee exposure balance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Step 4: If a Servicer still remains subject to the assessment of State foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: timeline compensatory fees after steps 1 through 3, the Servicer will be assessed its
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee exposure balance if not previously assessed. The Servicer will have
elements.label: text

elements.text: 90 days to submit any loan-level appeals in accordance with Section 9301.11(c)(iii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If after the appeals process is completed a Servicer continues to have a compensatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: fee exposure balance of greater than $300,000, then the Servicer will be billed for
elements.label: text

elements.text: such fees in accordance with Section 9301.11(c)(iv).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Servicer appeal process for State foreclosure timeline compensatory fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may appeal a pending compensatory fee prior to Freddie Mac billing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The calendar year’s estimated State foreclosure timeline compensatory fees will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: available in the Foreclosure Timeline Compensatory Fees Overview report, accessible via
elements.label: text

elements.text: the “Foreclosures” tile of the Servicer’s Servicer Performance Profile (SPP) (see Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 88, Servicing Tools). This report will be updated monthly, on the fifth Business Day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: each month to reflect the year-to-date State foreclosure timeline performance through the
elements.label: text

elements.text: end of the prior month.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are not required to submit appeals and it is recommended that Servicers do not
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit any appeals until all steps outlined in Section 9301.11(c)(ii) have been completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Servicer has been notified that the compensatory fees are now assessed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to billing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must submit, in their original appeal, a description of all delays along with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: related documentation. Any information that is received after the original appeal is
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted will not be considered.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer has 90 days to submit an appeal to Freddie Mac using the Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default Fee Appeal System (which can be accessed through Freddie Mac Gateway).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Billing process for compensatory fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Compensatory fees to be billed will appear on the Servicer’s Monthly Non-Performing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans Billing Statement in the first monthly cycle after the date in which all appeals have
elements.label: text

elements.text: been submitted to, and decisioned by, Freddie Mac. Refer to Section 9102.1 for
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on the payment of Servicing Non-Performing Loans Invoices via an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated Clearing House draft.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9401: Bankruptcy
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 9401.1: Bankruptcy overview, general requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: and Freddie Mac’s rights (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bankruptcy general requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter provides Servicers with Freddie Mac’s requirements for Servicing Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to bankruptcy proceedings or litigation. The Servicer must take appropriate action to
elements.label: text

elements.text: protect Freddie Mac’s interest during bankruptcy proceedings in which the Borrower is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: debtor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 9402 for requirements for Servicing Mortgages subject to other
elements.label: text

elements.text: litigation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to any other remedies it may have at law or in equity, Freddie Mac reserves the
elements.label: text

elements.text: right, at its sole discretion, to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Require the Servicer to submit copies of any and all records related to Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Require the Servicer to compensate Freddie Mac and hold Freddie Mac harmless for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: loss, damage or expense that result from the Servicer’s failure to comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions in this chapter, Chapters 8402 and 8403, Sections 9402.1(a) and 8601.7(a) or
elements.label: text

elements.text: that result from errors, omissions or delays by the Servicer or the Servicer’s agent,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including without limitation to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Previously paid incentives
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expense reimbursements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Court costs and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Attorney fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Use or require the Servicer to use Freddie Mac’s counsel or trustee for any Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default Legal Matter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Limit the amount of a reimbursement for attorney fees if those fees exceed the limits in:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exhibit 57B, Approved Bankruptcy Attorney Fees, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fees commonly charged for similar services in the area where the affected property is
elements.label: text

elements.text: located
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Limit the amount of a reimbursement for preservation and maintenance expenses if those
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses exceed the limits in Exhibit 57, 1- to 4-Unit Property Approved Expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. If the Servicer does not obtain Freddie Mac’s prior written approval as required, deny the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s request for reimbursement of expenses incurred as a result of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s Mortgages under the requirements of this chapter and Chapter 8402
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. If the Servicer fails to comply with the requirements contained in this chapter, Chapters
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8402 and 8403 and Sections 9402.1(a) and 8601.7(a):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assess compensatory and other fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exercise any remedies provided by the Guide and the other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Chapter 3602 regarding repurchases, repurchase alternatives and other remedies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Bankruptcy general requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Servicer receives notice or confirms information that a Borrower has filed a
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy petition, at a minimum, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Comply with all applicable laws and regulations, including working with the debtor’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: pre-bankruptcy credit counseling agency on a debt management plan, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Obtain a copy of the Borrower’s (debtor’s) bankruptcy petition or other bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Accurately complete and file a proof of claim, including all required proof of claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: forms, within the time limitations set by the bankruptcy court. This may include but is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to providing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Timely information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation (e.g., Borrower payment history) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff and reinstatement figures necessary for bankruptcy counsel to meet the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: limits set by the bankruptcy court if counsel is representing the Servicer in the case
elements.label: text

elements.text: If additional costs are incurred after confirmation of the bankruptcy plan, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must work with the trustee to ensure all steps are taken to recover those costs allowable
elements.label: text

elements.text: by applicable law from the debtor through the plan, if applicable. The Servicer must,
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to filing any proof of claim or motion for relief from the stay with respect to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage registered on the MERS® System, prepare and execute (using the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: employee who is a MERS authorized “signing officer”) an assignment of the Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument from MERS to the Servicer. The Servicer must record the prepared
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignment where required by State law. State mandated recordings are non-reimbursable
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac, are not considered part of the Freddie Mac allowable foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: counsel fees and must not be billed to the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Monitor the bankruptcy filing and obtain status of the proceedings from the trustee in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: timely manner. This includes, at a minimum, assisting with any motions for relief of stay
elements.label: text

elements.text: and monitoring the first meeting of creditors, proof of claim and/or confirmation of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy plan, pre- and post-petition payments, pleadings and notices. If counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: requests additional documentation and/or information from the Servicer at any time, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must provide such requested information and/or documents within three
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Days after receipt of the request, or within such earlier time frame if necessary,
elements.label: text

elements.text: to comply with timing requirements under applicable law or court orders and procedures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Maintain copies of all relevant documents related to the bankruptcy, including the notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: of first meeting of creditors, proof of claim, pleadings, notices, etc.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Determine whether the Borrower wishes to keep the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Not require the Borrower to sign a reaffirmation of debt agreement. If the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: chooses to have the Borrower reaffirm the debt, the Servicer must comply with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable laws, including obtaining the court’s approval of the reaffirmation agreement,
elements.label: text

elements.text: if necessary. Freddie Mac will not reimburse the Servicer for any legal costs incurred in
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtaining a reaffirmation agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Review any bankruptcy reorganization plan proposed under Chapters 11 or 13 of the U.S.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bankruptcy Code (refer to Section 9401.2(e) regarding the Servicer’s Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibilities should a bankruptcy judge order a bankruptcy cramdown) and respond in
elements.label: text

elements.text: a manner that protects Freddie Mac’s interests. Likewise, the Servicer must review any
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy plan proposed under Chapter 12 of the U.S. Bankruptcy Code and respond in
elements.label: text

elements.text: a manner that protects Freddie Mac’s interests. Such review includes verifying that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy repayment plan does not extend past the maturity date of the Mortgage and
elements.label: text

elements.text: sets forth the proper monthly payment to include the outstanding debt and sufficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds to pay property taxes and all property insurance and mortgage insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: when they become due. If the bankruptcy repayment plan will extend past the maturity
elements.label: text

elements.text: date of the Mortgage, the Servicer must object.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. If appropriate, file an action with the bankruptcy court to secure a determination that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property is abandoned. In the event the court considers the property abandoned, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must file an order lifting the automatic stay.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10. Monitor and properly apply payments received under any bankruptcy repayment plan. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower becomes delinquent in his or her payments under a bankruptcy repayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan, pursuant to Sections 9401.2(c) and 9401.2(d), as applicable, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: instruct counsel to take immediate action to modify the stay order and initiate or resume
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure proceedings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 11. Conduct a monthly inspection of the property for any delinquent Mortgage unless a
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy repayment plan is in place and being adhered to
elements.label: text

elements.text: 12. Verify that the Borrower is current on his or her property taxes and property and
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance premiums, as applicable. If there is an Escrow account, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must perform an Escrow analysis to determine if a bankruptcy repayment plan must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include additional Escrow Funds to maintain the Escrow account. In addition, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must perform a periodic Escrow analysis and notify the trustee of any change to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the payment amount resulting from the analysis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 13. Immediately notify the trustee of any Transfer of Servicing. (Both the Transferor Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Transferee Servicer must notify the trustee.) See Chapter 7101 for specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Transfers of Servicing, including Concurrent Transfers of Servicing and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subsequent Transfers of Servicing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 14. For leasehold Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If termination of the lease will impair Freddie Mac’s lien position or interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property, take appropriate action to assume the lease payments to the lessor if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: lease is rejected by the Chapter 7 trustee in bankruptcy and the Borrower ceases
elements.label: text

elements.text: making payments required under the terms of the lease (i.e., ground rents)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Object to any Chapter 13 plan that does not provide for payment of ground rents
elements.label: text

elements.text: 15. In cases when the trustee will pay post-petition payments, it is the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibility to notify the trustee of all changes, including any missed post-petition
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment, and to send copies of breach/acceleration letters to the trustee
elements.label: text

elements.text: 16. Immediately upon release of bankruptcy, if the Mortgage is delinquent, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Resume foreclosure activities if the Mortgage was in foreclosure previous to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy filing, in accordance with Chapter 9301, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Initiate or resume collection activity in accordance with Chapter 9102
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must inform the Borrower, Borrower’s counsel or bankruptcy trustee that an
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved modification is conditioned on obtaining the bankruptcy court’s approval, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary, to modify the Mortgage prior to the due date of the first modified payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9401.2: Managing bankruptcy filings and delinquency
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Multiple bankruptcy filings
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notifying Freddie Mac of bankruptcy proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Managing new bankruptcy filings
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Managing Mortgages that become delinquent subsequent to bankruptcy filing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bankruptcy cramdowns
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Multiple bankruptcy filings
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Identifying previous bankruptcy filings
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower may file for bankruptcy protection more than once or under different
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapters of the bankruptcy laws. When the Servicer receives notice of a Borrower filing a
elements.label: text

elements.text: petition for protection under the bankruptcy laws, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Check its Mortgage records to determine if the Borrower has previously filed for
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Refer the bankruptcy case to counsel if the Servicer’s records disclose a previous
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy filing for that Borrower and the Mortgage is delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Actions for bankruptcy filings
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must direct counsel to take appropriate action, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Borrower has filed multiple times but the bankruptcies are more than 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: apart:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Motion to annul the automatic stay to confirm foreclosure sale if the Servicer was
elements.label: text

elements.text: unaware that the Borrower had filed for bankruptcy and the Servicer had
elements.label: text

elements.text: conducted a foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Motion to dismiss the bankruptcy case if it can be shown that there has been no
elements.label: text

elements.text: substantial change in the Borrower’s financial circumstances since the last
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy filing or the Borrower has no prospect of repayment under a
elements.label: text

elements.text: reorganization plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Object to the confirmation of a Chapter 13 plan and a motion to dismiss in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with a Chapter 13 bankruptcy in which the reorganization plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: appears to be infeasible or offered in bad faith or it can be shown that there has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been no substantial change in the Borrower’s financial circumstances since the
elements.label: text

elements.text: last bankruptcy filing
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Motion for “in rem” relief or 180-day bar to prevent the Borrower from filing
elements.label: text

elements.text: another bankruptcy case in the future affecting the property securing Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s Mortgage when there are successive filings with a scheme of fraudulent
elements.label: text

elements.text: property transfers
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Perform any other actions as deemed appropriate and permitted under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bankruptcy Abuse Prevention and Consumer Protection Act of 2005
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the multiple filing is within 12 months of the previous filing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Object in the event that the Borrower petitions to extend the automatic stay:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the Borrower’s bankruptcy is presumptively filed not in good faith
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Bankruptcy Code
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the Servicer believes that the filing was offered in bad faith, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If there has been no substantial change in the Borrower’s financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances since the last bankruptcy filing. (See Exhibit 57B, Approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bankruptcy Attorney Fees, for the related allowable fee for filing an objection
elements.label: text

elements.text: to an extension of the automatic stay and attending the related court hearings.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Take any other actions as deemed appropriate and permitted under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bankruptcy Abuse Prevention and Consumer Protection Act of 2005
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Notifying Freddie Mac of bankruptcy proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report a bankruptcy filing to Freddie Mac, regardless of whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is current or delinquent in his or her Mortgage payments. The Servicer must submit
elements.label: text

elements.text: this notification via an EDR transmission within the first three Business Days of the month
elements.label: text

elements.text: following the month in which the Servicer received notice of the filing. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: also include the filing date and the applicable default action code (as listed below) to indicate
elements.label: text

elements.text: the bankruptcy chapter number.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 9301.11(b) and Exhibit 83A, Determining State Foreclosure Timeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: Performance Compensatory Fees, for allowable State foreclosure timeline delays related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower filing for bankruptcy protection.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The bankruptcy petition chapters and applicable default action codes are:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bankruptcy petition chapters and applicable default action codes
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bankruptcy chapter Default action code
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 7 65
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 11 66
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 12 59
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 13 67
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must also notify Freddie Mac when the court confirms the bankruptcy plan by
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting default action code 69 (Bankruptcy plan confirmed) or, in the event of a scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bankruptcy Cramdown, default action code of 35 (Bankruptcy Cramdown Scheduled) within
elements.label: text

elements.text: the first three Business Days of the month following the month in which the confirmation
elements.label: text

elements.text: occurred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must continue to report via an EDR transmission each month that the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: is in bankruptcy until the court clears the bankruptcy or lifts the stay. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: notify Freddie Mac within the first three Business Days of the month following the month in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which either of these actions occur via an EDR transmission using default action code 76
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Bankruptcy court clearance obtained/Stay lifted). The Servicer must include the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: action occurred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a bankruptcy converts from one chapter to another, the Servicer must report a default
elements.label: text

elements.text: action code 76 (Bankruptcy court clearance) to close the prior bankruptcy chapter. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: conversion date is the bankruptcy release date for the prior bankruptcy chapter and the filing
elements.label: text

elements.text: date for the new chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If a Chapter 13 converted to a Chapter 7 on 01/30/xx, the Servicer would report
elements.label: text

elements.text: default action code 76 using 01/30/xx to close the Chapter 13 status and a default action code
elements.label: text

elements.text: 65 using 01/30/xx to open the Chapter 7 bankruptcy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For additional information on EDR reporting requirements, refer to Section 9102.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Managing new bankruptcy filings
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) For Mortgages current at the time of filing
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower is current in his or her Mortgage payments at the time the Borrower files a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 7, 12 or 13 bankruptcy petition, the Servicer is not required to refer the matter to
elements.label: text

elements.text: counsel, and Freddie Mac will not reimburse the Servicer if the Servicer does so.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that special circumstances exist that require case management
elements.label: text

elements.text: by counsel on a current Mortgage (e.g., if the Servicer receives a proposed reorganization
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan that includes a bankruptcy cramdown), then the Servicer must obtain Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior written approval via e-mail (see Directory 5) to obtain counsel and to incur the
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal expense by submitting a request for pre-approval via PAID (Payments Automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools).
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Chapter 11 bankruptcy cases must be referred to counsel as soon as the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: receives notice that the Borrower has filed for bankruptcy protection, regardless of
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the Mortgage payments are current.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If applicable, refer to Section 9401.2(d) for requirements related to managing Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: that become delinquent subsequent to bankruptcy filing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) For Mortgages delinquent at the time of filing or for bankruptcy filings after a
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must refer the bankruptcy to counsel, whether a Chapter 7, 11, 12 or 13
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy petition, within three Business Days of the Servicer’s receipt of notice that a
elements.label: text

elements.text: petition has been filed if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower is at least 30 days delinquent in his or her Mortgage payments at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time bankruptcy is filed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower files a bankruptcy petition after the foreclosure sale and the bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: filing invalidates the foreclosure sale. (See Section 9301.10(a) for requirements on
elements.label: text

elements.text: requesting a rollback if the Servicer determines that the foreclosure sale is legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: invalid or void.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower files a bankruptcy petition after the foreclosure sale but prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expiration of a redemption, confirmation or ratification period. (See Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.10(a) regarding circumstances where it may be in Freddie Mac’s best interest to
elements.label: text

elements.text: process a rollback to allow the Servicer to report applicable information relating to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy repayment plan, if applicable.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for expenses the Servicer incurs in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Section 9401.4 and Chapter 9701.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must instruct counsel to file a motion for relief from the automatic stay:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In a Chapter 7 case:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Borrower is at least 60 days delinquent in his or her Mortgage payments at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the time of filing, at the same time the Servicer refers the case to counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Borrower is less than 60 days delinquent in his or her Mortgage payments at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the time of filing, no later than the 60th day of Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In a Chapter 11, 12, or 13 case, upon determining the Borrower became 60 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent in his or her post-petition and/or plan payments to either the Servicer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the trustee but no later than the 75th day of Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Managing Mortgages that become delinquent subsequent to bankruptcy filing
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower was current at the time of filing bankruptcy and subsequently becomes at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least 30 days delinquent in payments to either the Servicer or the trustee, then the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must refer the case to counsel. Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for expenses the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer incurs in accordance with Section 9401.4 and Chapter 9701.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must instruct counsel to file for relief from the automatic stay:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. In a Chapter 7 bankruptcy case, no later than the 60th day of Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. In a Chapter 12 or 13 bankruptcy case, upon determining the Borrower became 60 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent in his or her post-petition and/or plan payments to either the Servicer or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: trustee but no later than the 75th day of Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Chapter 11 bankruptcy cases already referred to counsel pursuant to Section 9401.2(c)(i),
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must instruct counsel to file for relief from the automatic stay upon determining
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower became 60 days delinquent in his or her post-petition and/or plan payments to
elements.label: text

elements.text: either the Servicer or the trustee but no later than the 75th day of Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Bankruptcy cramdowns
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Borrower files bankruptcy and the value of the property has declined to a value less
elements.label: text

elements.text: than the amount owed on the Mortgage, a federal bankruptcy judge may, in some instances,
elements.label: text

elements.text: order a division of the bankruptcy claim.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under this process, the court divides the Mortgage debt into two claims: a secured claim in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the amount of the current appraised value of the property and an unsecured claim for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: remaining balance of the debt.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All or a portion of the remaining unsecured claim balance is forgiven upon completion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: court-ordered repayment plan. This is known as a bankruptcy cramdown. Bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: cramdowns are not permitted on Mortgages secured by the Primary Residence of a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: who has filed a Chapter 13 bankruptcy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Notifying Freddie Mac of a proposed bankruptcy cramdown
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer receives a proposed reorganization plan that includes a bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: cramdown, the Servicer must advise its counsel to file an objection to the reorganization
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan. In doing so, the Servicer must direct its counsel to assert that the proposed plan may
elements.label: text

elements.text: not modify the original Security Instrument and Note by means of a bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: cramdown.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Servicer must complete and send a copy of Form 1155, Bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cramdown Pre-Confirmation Proposal of Settlement Terms, to notify Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 5) of the proposed plan within one Business Day of receiving the plan. Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will review the submitted Form 1155 and make a decision to approve or make a
elements.label: text

elements.text: counteroffer to the terms of the proposed plan. In some instances, Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: request additional information in its review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that all terms of the proposed plan are updated and correctly
elements.label: text

elements.text: stated on each amendment, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a proposed reorganization plan includes a bankruptcy cramdown that is not in
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with Freddie Mac workout options in Chapter 9204, the Servicer must object,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Freddie Mac will not agree to the terms of the reorganization plan. If the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: agrees to a reorganization plan without Freddie Mac’s written approval, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: reserves the right to exercise any remedies provided by the Guide and the other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents. Refer to Chapter 3602 regarding repurchases, repurchase alternatives and
elements.label: text

elements.text: other remedies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Notifying Freddie Mac of a confirmed bankruptcy cramdown
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a reorganization plan that includes a bankruptcy cramdown has been confirmed, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of the confirmed plan within one Business Day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: receiving the plan. In doing so, the Servicer must transmit the final terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: confirmed bankruptcy cramdown (a “bankruptcy cramdown modification”) to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac via the custom modification screen in Resolve® as follows.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A) Entering the data into Resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: By completing and submitting the custom modification template in Resolve and
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitting the bankruptcy cramdown modification for settlement, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: represents and warrants to Freddie Mac that it has completed the data entry in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the instructions set forth in Resolve Online Help and that all
elements.label: text

elements.text: information set forth in the custom modification template is accurate and in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the terms of the confirmed plan. In submitting the bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: cramdown modification via the custom modification template, the Servicer represents
elements.label: text

elements.text: and warrants, among other things, that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The data entered on the custom modification template matches the final terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the confirmed bankruptcy cramdown
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The property value is either the BPO or appraisal, depending on what was
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by the court
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Modification pending update report and modification status
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the data entered in the custom modification template has been submitted to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, Servicers should monitor the Modification Pending Update report,
elements.label: text

elements.text: accessible via the “Modifications” tile of the Servicer’s Servicer Performance Profile
elements.label: text

elements.text: (SPP) (see Exhibit 88). All Mortgages that are scheduled to be processed in Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s systems will appear on this report in the SPP.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, Freddie Mac will notify Servicers that the bankruptcy cramdown
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification has been processed in Freddie Mac’s systems via the Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Status Overview report in the SPP. If a Servicer attempts to report a monthly loan￾level transaction on a Mortgage based on the modified terms prior to the bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: cramdown modification being processed in Freddie Mac’s systems, the Servicer will
elements.label: text

elements.text: not be able to successfully complete the transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Other requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that proper EDR requirements are followed in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9401.2(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon determining the Borrower became 60 days delinquent in his or her payments under
elements.label: text

elements.text: a confirmed reorganization plan that includes a bankruptcy cramdown but no later than
elements.label: text

elements.text: the 75th day of Delinquency, the Servicer must instruct counsel to file for relief from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: automatic stay. (See Sections 9401.2(c) and 9401.2(d), as applicable, regarding filing for
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief from the automatic stay.) For any other default under the terms of a confirmed
elements.label: text

elements.text: reorganization plan that includes a bankruptcy cramdown, the Servicer must notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) within one Business Day of such default and indicate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: type of default and include the Servicer’s recommendation as to how to proceed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9401.3: Servicing bankruptcy counsel and other
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Servicing requirements (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing bankruptcies on Mortgages secured by income-producing properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Selecting bankruptcy counsel, bankruptcy referrals and the Servicer’s responsibility to work
elements.label: text

elements.text: with bankruptcy counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicing bankruptcies on Mortgages secured by income-producing properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower on a Mortgage secured by income-producing property files a bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: petition and Freddie Mac has an assignment of rents, the Servicer must take action to prohibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower from using any income from the property for any purpose other than making
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments on the Mortgage by instructing counsel to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ File a motion for sequestration of rental income, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain an adequate protection order
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Selecting bankruptcy counsel, bankruptcy referrals and the Servicer’s responsibility to
elements.label: text

elements.text: work with bankruptcy counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Selecting bankruptcy counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: For requirements applicable to Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters, which include
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy matters, refer to Chapter 9501.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Bankruptcy referrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower is referred to foreclosure after the bankruptcy referral, the Servicer is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: required to refer the foreclosure to the same law firm that handled the bankruptcy matter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, the Servicer should give preference to the law firm that handled the bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: matter if the Servicer determines it is in Freddie Mac’s best interest.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower is in foreclosure at the time of the bankruptcy referral or files a
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy petition after the foreclosure sale (see Section 9401.2(c) regarding managing
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy filings after a foreclosure sale), the Servicer is not required to refer the
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy case to the same law firm handling the foreclosure. However, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: should give preference to the law firm handling the foreclosure if the Servicer determines
elements.label: text

elements.text: it is in Freddie Mac’s best interest.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer does not refer the bankruptcy case to the law firm handling the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, then the Servicer must ensure that it periodically updates the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: counsel regarding the status of the bankruptcy matter. Once the bankruptcy matter is
elements.label: text

elements.text: resolved, the Servicer is responsible for transitioning the foreclosure back to the law firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: handling the foreclosure for any further required proceedings immediately upon release
elements.label: text

elements.text: of bankruptcy but no later than one Business Day after the resolution of the bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: matter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When referring a bankruptcy case to counsel, the Servicer must provide all
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation and/or information required to handle the bankruptcy matter and work
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: with the bankruptcy attorney to facilitate either timely reinstatement or relief from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: automatic stay.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Servicer’s responsibility to work with bankruptcy counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Servicer refers a bankruptcy case to counsel, the Servicer must provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete written reinstatement or payoff figures to the attorney, trustee, workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: specialist or outsourcing vendor, as applicable, at the time of referral unless the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: is in foreclosure at the time of the bankruptcy referral and the Servicer refers the
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy case to the law firm handling the foreclosure. Also, the Servicer must respond
elements.label: text

elements.text: to any additional requests for reinstatement and/or payoff figures after referral within two
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Days of the date on which the Servicer receives a written request. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may provide the written reinstatement or payoff figures via a paper document, facsimile
elements.label: text

elements.text: or e-mail.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the bankruptcy counsel requests any additional information and/or documentation at
elements.label: text

elements.text: any time, the Servicer must provide such requested information and/or documents within
elements.label: text

elements.text: three Business Days after receipt of the request or such earlier time frame if necessary to
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with timing requirements under applicable law or court orders and procedures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must maintain communication with the bankruptcy attorney so that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer can manage the bankruptcy case effectively. No less than one time per month,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must require the bankruptcy attorney to report the status of the case, request
elements.label: text

elements.text: any documentation needed from the Servicer and report any relevant information to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer. The Servicer must maintain this information in the Mortgage file or on its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage system.

elements.text: 9401.4 Reimbursement of expenses related to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Attorney fees and cost recovery in bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimbursement and pre-approval by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conditions for non-reimbursement of attorney fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Attorney fees and cost recovery in bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: When legally permissible, the Servicer must attempt to obtain payment of the attorney fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: and costs resulting from the bankruptcy proceedings directly from the Borrower and/or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy court.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reimbursement and pre-approval by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for expenses the Servicer incurs in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the limits listed in Exhibit 57B, Approved Bankruptcy Attorney Fees, and according to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements contained in Chapter 9701. The Servicer must obtain Freddie Mac’s written
elements.label: text

elements.text: pre-approval for any fees in excess of the fees listed in Exhibit 57B by submitting a request
elements.label: text

elements.text: for pre-approval via PAID (Payments Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Tools).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Conditions for non-reimbursement of attorney fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not reimburse Servicers for attorney fees or legal costs on any bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: case if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower was current on the Mortgage at the time the Borrower filed for bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: protection and has remained current and if Freddie Mac did not approve a referral to
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy counsel for special circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer incurred costs for reaffirmation agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer incorrectly reported or failed to report the bankruptcy through EDR. See
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9401.2(b) for more details on how to report bankruptcies through EDR.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm was not eligible to receive referrals of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified in Chapter 9501

elements.text: Chapter 9402: Other Litigation Involving Freddie
