elements.text: 9402.1: Freddie Mac rights and litigation types
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Routine and non-routine litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Freddie Mac rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to direct and control all litigation involving a Freddie Mac￾owned or guaranteed Mortgage, regardless of whether Freddie Mac is a named party. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer and all law firms handling the litigation, whether selected and engaged pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9501 or Section 8601.5(g) or 9402.2(b), must cooperate fully with Freddie Mac in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the prosecution, defense and handling of the matter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9401.1(b) for Freddie Mac rights when the requirements of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section regarding Servicer and law firm cooperation with Freddie Mac in the prosecution,
elements.label: text

elements.text: defense and handling of litigation involving Freddie Mac-owned or guaranteed Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: are not met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Routine and non-routine litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Definition of routine and non-routine litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer should be familiar with the following definitions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Definitions of routine and non-routine litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: N
elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-routine
elements.label: text

elements.text: litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: A contested action in which the Borrower alleges case-specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: defenses or issues that, if successful, would create negative legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: precedent beyond the immediate case
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9402
elements.label: text

elements.text: Definitions of routine and non-routine litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: R
elements.label: text

elements.text: Routine
elements.label: text

elements.text: litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: A contested action in which the Borrower alleges case-specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: defenses or issues that, if successful, would not create negative
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal precedent beyond the immediate case
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of non-routine litigation that must be reported to Freddie Mac as non-routine
elements.label: text

elements.text: litigation include, but are not limited to, the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actions that name Freddie Mac as a party
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actions that seek monetary relief against Freddie Mac, including any claim (including
elements.label: text

elements.text: counterclaims, cross-claims or third-party claims in foreclosure or bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: actions) for damages against Freddie Mac or its officers, directors or employees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actions that challenge the validity, priority or enforceability of a Freddie Mac-owned
elements.label: text

elements.text: or guaranteed Mortgage or seek to impair Freddie Mac’s interest in an REO,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, by way of example:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ An action seeking to demolish a structure on the property or the property as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: result of a code violation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ An action seeking to avoid a lien based on a failure to comply with a law or
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ An attempt by a junior lienholder to assert priority over a Freddie Mac-owned or
elements.label: text

elements.text: guaranteed Mortgage or extinguish Freddie Mac’s interests
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A quiet title action seeking to declare Freddie Mac’s lien void; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ An attempt by a Borrower to effect a cramdown of a Mortgage in bankruptcy as
elements.label: text

elements.text: to which Freddie Mac has not delegated authority to the Servicer or law firm to
elements.label: text

elements.text: address
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actions that present an issue that may pose significant legal or reputational risk to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac include, by way of example:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any issue involving Freddie Mac’s conservatorship; its conservator, FHFA;
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s status as a federal instrumentality; or an interpretation of Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s charter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any assertion that Freddie Mac is a federal agency or otherwise part of the U.S.
elements.label: text

elements.text: government
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9402
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any “due process” or other constitutional challenge
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any challenge to the methods by which Freddie Mac does business
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any putative class actions involving a Freddie Mac-owned or guaranteed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Challenges to the standing of the Servicer to conduct foreclosures or bankruptcies
elements.label: text

elements.text: that, if successful, could create negative legal precedent with an impact beyond
elements.label: text

elements.text: the immediate case
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Challenges to the methods by which MERS® does business or its ability to act as
elements.label: text

elements.text: nominee under a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any “show cause orders” or motions for sanctions relating to a Freddie Mac￾owned or guaranteed Mortgage, whether against Freddie Mac, the Servicer, a law
elements.label: text

elements.text: firm or a vendor of the Servicer or law firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any appellate or other action for post-judgment relief in any foreclosure,
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy or legal action in which Freddie Mac is a named party
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Foreclosures on HUD-Guaranteed Section 184 Native American Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any environmental litigation relating to a Freddie Mac-owned or guaranteed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A need to foreclose judicially in a State where non-judicial foreclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: predominate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any claim brought by a governmental body
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Cross-border insolvency proceedings under Chapter 15 of the Bankruptcy Code
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any claim of predatory lending or discrimination in Mortgage origination or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any claim implicating the interpretation of the terms of the Fannie Mae/Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Uniform Instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actions involving an eNote or eMortgage (as those terms are defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Given the evolving nature of default-related litigation, it is not possible to provide an
elements.label: text

elements.text: exhaustive list of non-routine litigation. Each contested action presents unique
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances, and the Servicer should evaluate each action on a case-by-case basis to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine whether a contested action is routine or non-routine.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9402
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Legal actions and strategies initiated by the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must obtain written approval (see Directory 5) from the Freddie Mac Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Division prior to initiating the following legal actions and strategies:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filing a new legal action, other than a Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter, on behalf of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filing a motion to intervene in a pending legal action on behalf of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appealing or otherwise challenging a judgment in any foreclosure or bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeding or any legal action in which Freddie Mac is a named party
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filing a notice of removal to federal district court for any legal action in which
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac is a named party
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Asserting any position in a legal action that relates to Freddie Mac’s status as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: government-sponsored enterprise, its conservatorship, or its conservator, FHFA
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Propounding discovery requests or otherwise serving or providing any discovery
elements.label: text

elements.text: responses on behalf of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Referring to Freddie Mac in litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac must be described in legal proceedings as “Federal Home Loan Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation (“Freddie Mac”), a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: United States of America.” Freddie Mac may not be referred to as a “government
elements.label: text

elements.text: agency.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)MERS-registered Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 8101.5(b) for additional requirements relating to notices from MERS and
elements.label: text

elements.text: MERS-registered Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9402.2: Litigation reporting, notification and legal
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: representation (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting and notification requirements for litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Counsel retained by Servicers pursuant to Servicer’s duty to indemnify Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9402
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Reporting and notification requirements for litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) EDR requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must monitor all Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters and act without delay
elements.label: text

elements.text: and in accordance with applicable law when responding to any litigation matter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If litigation involves a Mortgage that is 30 or more days delinquent, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: report the litigation matter to Freddie Mac via EDR within the first three Business Days
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the month following the month in which the Servicer became aware of the litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: using default action code 33 (Contested Foreclosure and Litigation).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Legal reporting requirements for non-routine litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Notifying Freddie Mac of non-routine litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must act without delay and notify Freddie Mac within two Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days of determining that the Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter involves or evolves
elements.label: text

elements.text: into non-routine litigation. All notifications must be sent via e-mail (see Directory 5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer retains counsel not selected and engaged pursuant to Chapter 9501 to
elements.label: text

elements.text: handle the non-routine litigation matter (see Sections 8601.5(g) and 9402.2(b)), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: notification to Freddie Mac must include the name, address, phone number and e￾mail address of the Servicer’s counsel and a brief summary, including but not limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The issues presented,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Freddie Mac loan number,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The docket number,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The case caption and court, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any relevant pleadings
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once Freddie Mac has been notified accordingly, the Servicer and the law firm(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: handling the non-routine litigation matter must periodically update Freddie Mac on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the progress of the litigation. Freddie Mac must be provided sufficient opportunity,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but no less than five Business Days before the filing deadline, to review and
elements.label: text

elements.text: comment upon any substantive pleadings, including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Motions,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Replies,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Briefs, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9402
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Proposed orders
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Notification of a modification of a Mortgage subject to active non-routine
elements.label: text

elements.text: litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is subject to active non-routine litigation, and the Servicer determines
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower is eligible for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification®, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conduct a review through its in-house or outside counsel to determine that it is
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate to send the Trial Period Plan offer per applicable laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that any active litigation and settlement negotiations by Servicer’s counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: are not undermined and that Freddie Mac’s interests are not adversely impacted
elements.label: text

elements.text: If it is deemed appropriate to send based upon the aforementioned review, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer or the Servicer’s counsel must send the Trial Period Plan offer to an eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s counsel or, if not represented, to the Borrower directly when that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Mortgage is subject to active non-routine litigation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Legal expenses for non-routine litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain Freddie Mac’s prior written approval before incurring any
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses in the Servicing of a non-routine litigation matter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To obtain Freddie Mac’s approval, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contact Freddie Mac with details of the non-routine litigation matter (see Directory
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5) according to the reporting requirements in Section 9402.2(a)(ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submit a request for pre-approval (RPA) in PAID (Payments Automated Intelligent
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) for all non-routine litigation legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses that are reimbursable to the Servicer. The RPA submission must include an
elements.label: text

elements.text: estimate of the attorney’s fees and litigation costs and the attorney’s hourly rate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a delay in taking protective action might result in the impairment of the property or
elements.label: text

elements.text: jeopardize Freddie Mac’s lien position, the Servicer must immediately submit an RPA. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: unusual or emergency circumstances do not allow the Servicer to request Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior written approval, then the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via PAID by the next
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day after the Servicer incurred the expense. If the Servicer’s determination to
elements.label: text

elements.text: incur the expense was reasonable, as determined in Freddie Mac’s sole discretion,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Counsel retained by Servicers pursuant to Servicer’s duty to indemnify Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Servicer responsibilities for retaining counsel in litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Service Guide Chapter 9402
elements.label: text

elements.text: From time to time, the Servicer may retain counsel to represent Freddie Mac and/or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer in litigation involving allegations that, if true, could subject the Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: liability to Freddie Mac for a failure to comply with any selling or Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation or warranty or requirement of the Guide or other Purchase Documents. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: these cases:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the Servicer retains counsel for this purpose, the Servicer remains liable for
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal fees and costs incurred in the defense of any litigation, as well as any and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: losses, judgments, damages and expenses, including fees and costs entered against
elements.label: text

elements.text: and incurred on behalf of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for Freddie Mac’s proportionate share of
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses for responding to Borrower defenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Counsel selection and notification requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Counsel retained and paid by Servicers pursuant to Section 9402.1(b) and this section do
elements.label: text

elements.text: not need to be selected and engaged pursuant to Chapter 9501. For all other Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default Legal Matters, the Servicer must use counsel selected and engaged pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9501.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) of its retention of counsel not
elements.label: text

elements.text: selected and engaged pursuant to Chapter 9501 and are required to comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting requirements in Section 9402.2(a)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Litigation management and transitioning legal matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that the law firm to which the Freddie Mac Default Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Matter was originally referred is updated on the current status of the litigation either by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer or its new counsel. Unless the entire legal matter or action is resolved, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is responsible for transitioning the Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter back to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: law firm for further proceedings. This transition must occur no later than one Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Day after the resolution of the litigation matter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Borrower responsibility for legal fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Security Instrument provides for the Borrower to reimburse the Servicer for
elements.label: text

elements.text: any legal fees and costs incurred, the Servicer should:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Instruct its counsel to notify the Borrower about his or her responsibility for such
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure its counsel resolve such matters through stipulation or any other expeditious
elements.label: text

elements.text: manner that will minimize fees and costs to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9501: Selection, Retention and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Management of Law Firms for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9501.1: Firm management and evaluation (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer’s management of law firms for Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Review and evaluation of firms
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicer’s management of law firms for Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter sets forth requirements for the Servicer’s review and evaluation, selection,
elements.label: text

elements.text: retention and management of law firms (referred to throughout this chapter as “firms”) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Servicer is responsible for retaining firms for Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will continue to retain firms directly for REO-related legal services: eviction,
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO closing and related litigation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapters 9401 and 9402 for more information relating to litigation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Review and evaluation of firms
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Servicer due diligence
elements.label: text

elements.text: As part of its selection process, each Servicer is responsible for obtaining and evaluating
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation and information from firms and conducting due diligence to ensure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: selected firms meet the requirements set forth in Section 9501.2. As part of the process,
elements.label: text

elements.text: each Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain and review all required documentation and information submitted by each
elements.label: text

elements.text: firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that it selects from a pool of potentially acceptable firms that is diverse and
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes minority and women-owned firms and other diverse firms when feasible;
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that the firm or any entity or individual performing work for the firm is not on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Freddie Mac Exclusionary List in accordance with Section 3101.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Due diligence documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide to Freddie Mac upon request a copy of each firm’s application
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and related due diligence documentation. Freddie Mac reserves the right to
elements.label: text

elements.text: review the process, procedures and due diligence used by the Servicer to evaluate and
elements.label: text

elements.text: select a firm.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Document retention requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must retain all information submitted by a firm in support of the firm’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: application and all information otherwise gathered by the Servicer regarding the firm.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer retains the firm to provide legal services on Freddie Mac-owned or
elements.label: text

elements.text: guaranteed Mortgages, then it must retain the information for as long as the firm provides
elements.label: text

elements.text: those legal services plus the longer of any retention period applicable to the Servicer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: seven years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer does not retain the firm to provide legal services on Freddie Mac-owned or
elements.label: text

elements.text: guaranteed Mortgages, then it must retain the information for the longer of any retention
elements.label: text

elements.text: period applicable to the Servicer or seven years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9501.2: Firm minimum requirements (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Standards and practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Firm structure and resources
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Experience and expertise
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compliance and ethics
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Management and oversight
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Technology and reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Diversity and communication
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that all firms selected and retained to handle Freddie Mac Default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Legal Matters meet the firm minimum requirements specified in this section (“Firm Minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements”) and all other applicable Freddie Mac requirements. The Firm Minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements are as follows.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Standards and practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Firm practice
elements.label: text

elements.text: The firm’s practice areas must include end-to-end:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Default-related legal services: foreclosure, bankruptcy, loss mitigation (e.g., deeds-in￾lieu of foreclosure), default-related litigation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ REO-related legal services: eviction, REO closing and related litigation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The firm must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be familiar with industry standards in the jurisdiction in which it practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Understand the jurisdiction legal processes and requirements in default-related and
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO-related legal services; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Understand the substantive legal issues in the jurisdiction (e.g., standing)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Servicer must consider firm experience in the following areas:
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure mediation, the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act, title curative issues and
elements.label: text

elements.text: general housing-related issues (e.g., rent control, Section 8, lead paint liability, health
elements.label: text

elements.text: code violations, foreclosure redemption, confirmation and ratification, condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: associations, homeowners associations (HOAs), mobile home matters or Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Share Loans (see Chapter 8801 for special Servicing requirements for Cooperative Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans)). The firm should also have some experience with delegation for loss mitigation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must also consider the firm’s membership in default-related and REO￾related trade and industry groups, attendance or participation in State bar associations,
elements.label: text

elements.text: seminar and lecture participation and attendance and any other activities relevant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: default-related and REO-related law practice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Timelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must review the firm’s completion timelines and confirm that the firm is
elements.label: text

elements.text: able to track, monitor and complete foreclosure and bankruptcy matters in compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with applicable law and Freddie Mac timeline requirements, taking into consideration
elements.label: text

elements.text: outside factors that impact compliance with Freddie Mac timelines such as new
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure requirements and court delays.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Information privacy
elements.label: text

elements.text: The firm must maintain physical, technical and procedural controls and effective
elements.label: text

elements.text: information security and data management to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure the security and confidentiality of personally identifiable information (PII)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and confidential information, whether in paper, electronic or other form
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Protect against any threats or hazards to the security or integrity of such information;
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Protect against unauthorized access to or use of such information
elements.label: text

elements.text: The firm must implement controls meeting or exceeding industry standards, including, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, standards promulgated by the International Office for Standardization or
elements.label: text

elements.text: National Institute for Standards and Technology. Specifically:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm must ensure that PII that is stored on the firm’s systems and workstations is
elements.label: text

elements.text: encrypted at rest at all times
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm must have secured storage for promissory notes and other original
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents to prevent theft and to ensure protection against fire, flood or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: damage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm may not perform, outsource or send to any affiliate outside of the United
elements.label: text

elements.text: States or its territories any legal work on Freddie Mac-owned or guaranteed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, including any storage of Freddie Mac data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm may not send any PII underlying Freddie Mac-owned or guaranteed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages outside the United States
elements.label: text

elements.text: The firm must have written policies, procedures and processes in place by the date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission of the Servicer Selection Form related to protection of PII and fraud
elements.label: text

elements.text: prevention, including policies, procedures and processes related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Background checks of all employees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Protection of PII
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fraud prevention and identification; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Incident response and notification protocols for data breaches and other security
elements.label: text

elements.text: incidents
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must review and confirm that the firm meets these requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: information security, data management, protection of PII and fraud prevention.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Firm structure and resources
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) One staffed office with physical presence in jurisdiction
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the law firm has at least one appropriately staffed office
elements.label: text

elements.text: physically located in a jurisdiction in which the firm is retained for Freddie Mac Default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Legal Matters. An appropriately staffed office means a physical brick and mortar office at
elements.label: text

elements.text: which law firm employees regularly work that is not a place of residence and that
elements.label: text

elements.text: contains the usual equipment and facilities necessary to operate a law practice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A law firm must be registered, licensed and/or permitted, as necessary, with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate jurisdictional authorities for all jurisdictions in which it practices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Jurisdiction-wide coverage and use of local counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that the firm has the ability to cover foreclosure, bankruptcy,
elements.label: text

elements.text: eviction, REO closing matters and default-related litigation throughout the jurisdiction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the firm has partnerships or relationships with third parties (e.g., local counsel, trustee
elements.label: text

elements.text: companies or title companies) that will perform or complete some aspect of the default￾related and REO-related work, the Servicer must require the firm to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain disclosure from the firm regarding such relationships and the extent to which
elements.label: text

elements.text: third parties will be relied upon, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determine whether the firm has a reasonable contingency plan for the loss of any of
elements.label: text

elements.text: those relationships or operational processes
elements.label: text

elements.text: In evaluating any such third-party relationship, the Servicer must consider the length of
elements.label: text

elements.text: time the relationship has existed and the adequacy of the firm’s written policies to
elements.label: text

elements.text: mitigate third-party risk.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a firm uses local counsel to handle matters within the jurisdiction, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure that the firm has a process to select, manage and review the local counsel and their
elements.label: text

elements.text: work product. The process must be designed to ensure that local attorneys are qualified
elements.label: text

elements.text: and adequately trained and have a satisfactory history with respect to bar complaints,
elements.label: text

elements.text: sanctions and similar matters.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a firm’s contested caseload (e.g., contested foreclosures and litigated cases), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: firm’s reliance on local counsel must be minimal. Any use of local counsel for these
elements.label: text

elements.text: matters must be structured so that the retained firm will direct and manage the local
elements.label: text

elements.text: counsel on those matters.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Firm capacity
elements.label: text

elements.text: As of the date of the submission of the Servicer Selection Form via
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://www.freddiemacsats.com/Security/Login, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Confirm that the firm has the ability to accept additional referrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Confirm that the firm is not operating at full capacity, given the existing facilities,
elements.label: text

elements.text: personnel and technology. Alternatively, the firm must outline to the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: satisfaction the steps and time frame necessary to be in a position to handle additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: referrals while still maintaining appropriate firm-to-file and staff-to-file ratios.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Confirm that the firm has contingency plans to deal with a contraction in the market
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Insurance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the firm has an appropriate level of malpractice and errors
elements.label: text

elements.text: and omissions insurance coverage in place or be able to obtain an appropriate amount of
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance by the date of the submission of the Servicer Selection Form. The appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: level of insurance coverage will depend upon the total number of Freddie Mac and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae files the firm is managing or expects to manage when being evaluated by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer. The firm must have the ability to obtain the appropriate amount of insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage under the requirements as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tier I: Volume of 0–4,499 foreclosure matters, coverage of not less than $1 million
elements.label: text

elements.text: per occurrence with an aggregate of not less than $3 million
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tier II: Volume of 4,500–19,999 foreclosure matters, coverage of not less than $5
elements.label: text

elements.text: million per occurrence with an aggregate of not less than $5 million; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tier III: Volume of 20,000 or more foreclosure matters, coverage of not less than $8
elements.label: text

elements.text: million per occurrence with an aggregate of not less than $8 million
elements.label: text

elements.text: The required level of insurance is determined by the higher of the Freddie Mac or Fannie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mae pending foreclosure volume.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If a firm had 2,000 Freddie Mac foreclosure matters and 4,501 Fannie Mae
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure matters, the firm would fall within Tier II, and the required coverage would
elements.label: text

elements.text: be not less than $5 million per occurrence with an aggregate of not less than $5 million.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Beginning in 2014, Servicers must conduct an updated coverage analysis annually, with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appropriate level of insurance to be determined by the number of matters being
elements.label: text

elements.text: handled as of June 1 of each year. When an annual review reveals a need to increase a
elements.label: text

elements.text: firm’s coverage, firms will have until December 31 of each year to obtain any required
elements.label: text

elements.text: increased coverage. Servicers may grant firms additional time to obtain increased
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage, if necessary, to reach the routine renewal date for the firm’s policy but may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: grant extensions beyond June 1 of the following year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Financial resources
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the firm has adequate financial resources and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: financial ability to make required advances in connection with filing fees and costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary to process default-related and REO-related matters.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must review the firm’s financial statements and/or other firm financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents in order to confirm that the firm has sufficient reserves or credit lines to
elements.label: text

elements.text: manage operating expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Business continuity
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the firm has business continuity and/or disaster recovery
elements.label: text

elements.text: plans in place to recover critical business functions. The firm must have a documented
elements.label: text

elements.text: succession/continuity plan in the event of loss of the firm owners/partners.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Experience and expertise
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Firm has adequate, relevant jurisdiction-specific experience
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that a law firm has at least two full-time attorneys dedicated
elements.label: text

elements.text: to default Mortgage practice in each jurisdiction in which the law firm has been selected
elements.label: text

elements.text: and retained for default-related legal services (the “Qualifying Attorneys”), with special
elements.label: text

elements.text: rules applicable to select jurisdictions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The law firm must have at least two Qualifying Attorneys in each jurisdiction in which
elements.label: text

elements.text: the law firm has been selected and retained for default-related legal services, one with at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least eight years and a second with at least five years of jurisdiction-specific experience
elements.label: text

elements.text: in:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Foreclosure (including, where applicable, confirmation, redemption and ratification
elements.label: text

elements.text: matters)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loss mitigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eviction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ REO closing and related litigation (collectively, “default Mortgage practice”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Qualifying Attorney must be an owner, partner or full-time employee of the firm.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The law firm can meet the requirement that the Qualifying Attorneys are “in each
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdiction” in one of two ways:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the law firm does not have an appropriately staffed and equipped office in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdiction, the two Qualifying Attorneys must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Reside in the relevant jurisdiction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be licensed and in good standing to practice law in the relevant jurisdiction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Practice law on a full-time basis, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Devote more than fifty percent (50%) of their working time to default Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: practice in the relevant jurisdiction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the law firm has an appropriately staffed and equipped office in the jurisdiction,
elements.label: text

elements.text: one or both of the two Qualifying Attorneys may reside outside of the jurisdiction,
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided the Qualifying Attorneys not residing in the relevant jurisdiction ordinarily
elements.label: text

elements.text: work out of the firm’s office(s) in the relevant jurisdiction (except during situations
elements.label: text

elements.text: involving national or regional emergencies such as natural disasters, health-related
elements.label: text

elements.text: pandemics or epidemics and other situations making it appropriate and reasonable for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the firm to not require employees to report for work at the firm’s office(s))
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain a list of the names of the firm’s managing attorneys, partners
elements.label: text

elements.text: and associates with the years of experience in each area (foreclosure, bankruptcy,
elements.label: text

elements.text: eviction, REO closing and related litigation).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Special rules apply to the following jurisdictions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The States of Alaska, Idaho, Montana, New Hampshire, North Dakota, Rhode Island,
elements.label: text

elements.text: South Dakota, West Virginia and Wyoming
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The U.S. territories of Guam, Puerto Rico, Samoa and the U.S. Virgin Islands
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The District of Columbia
elements.label: text

elements.text: In these jurisdictions, the two Qualifying Attorneys must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be licensed and in good standing to practice law in the relevant jurisdiction, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Practice law on a full-time basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: While Freddie Mac expects that Servicers will give preference to firms that employ at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least two attorneys who reside in the relevant jurisdiction and devote more than fifty
elements.label: text

elements.text: percent (50%) of their working time to default Mortgage practice in the relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdiction, Servicers may use a firm that does not meet these two standards if the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise meets the Firm Minimum Requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When submitting the Servicer Selection Form pursuant to Section 9501.3(a)(i), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must identify the Qualifying Attorneys for each relevant jurisdiction. At a
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify where the attorneys reside by city or county
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify the jurisdictions in which the attorneys are licensed and are in good standing
elements.label: text

elements.text: to practice law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ State whether the attorneys practice law on a full-time basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Describe the employment relationship between the firm and the attorneys, such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the attorneys are owners, principals, members, shareholders, partners,
elements.label: text

elements.text: associates or other employees
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Prior volume experience
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must confirm the firm and/or managing attorney(s) has completed a sufficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: number of foreclosure, bankruptcy, loss mitigation, eviction and REO matters within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: past 24 months to demonstrate that the firm has experience in representing creditors in
elements.label: text

elements.text: default-related matters.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the 24-month period, the Servicer must review the total number of matters referred,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the total number of matters completed and the number of matters currently pending for
elements.label: text

elements.text: each of the following areas: foreclosure, bankruptcy, loss mitigation, eviction and REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: closing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: What constitutes a sufficient number of completed default-related and REO-related legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: services will vary depending upon the jurisdiction at issue, the volume the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: expects to refer to the firm and the relative size of the firm. Servicers must consider these
elements.label: text

elements.text: factors when making this determination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Jurisdiction-specific industry references
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain from the firm at least two jurisdiction-specific Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers or default-related references, or, if the firm has been in existence less than one
elements.label: text

elements.text: year, the partners or shareholders of the firm must provide at least two Servicer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: default-related references in connection with work performed in the particular
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdiction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Staff experience
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must determine whether the firm’s non-attorney staff has reasonable
elements.label: text

elements.text: experience. In determining what constitutes reasonable experience, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: consider the average years of experience, education, qualifications and demonstrated
elements.label: text

elements.text: ability of the non-attorney staff in relation to their respective levels of responsibility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Compliance and ethics
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Attorney licensing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the firm’s attorneys who will handle Freddie Mac Default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Legal Matters are licensed to practice and in good standing in the jurisdiction in which
elements.label: text

elements.text: the firm is being retained. Legal work must be performed by attorneys licensed in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdiction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Ethics and professional standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: The firm must demonstrate a history of legal practice that comports with applicable legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: and ethical standards and reflects high professional standards. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: conclude that the firm does not, in the totality of the circumstances, pose a legal and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: reputational risk or exhibit systematic issues that may lead to reputational and/or legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: risk to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain the following information from the firm in order to evaluate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficiency of the firm’s professional standards:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any sanctions against the firm or any of its present or former attorneys in the past
elements.label: text

elements.text: five years, including the nature of the sanctions and if they relate to a loan-level
elements.label: text

elements.text: matter or systemic firm practice, and, if related to firm practice, any corrective actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: taken by the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any bar complaints/reprimands against present and former firm attorneys in the past
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10 years and whether the complaints were closed, are pending or resulted in some
elements.label: text

elements.text: form of adverse action
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any government investigations involving firm practices in the past 10 years and
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the investigations involved firm practices or are related to client
elements.label: text

elements.text: investigations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any damages or settlement of claims as a result of an allegation of professional
elements.label: text

elements.text: negligence against the firm or its attorneys in the past five years (i) in excess of
elements.label: text

elements.text: $20,000 in any single occurrence and $50,000 in the aggregate, or (ii) reflect a
elements.label: text

elements.text: possible pattern of professional negligence, regardless of amount; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any significant litigation asserting systemic issues with firm processes or legal work,
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as any class-action lawsuit against the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is aware of any of the above items that involve the firm’s professional
elements.label: text

elements.text: standards but which were not disclosed by the firm, the Servicer must disclose them to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac in the Servicer Selection Form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain a disclosure from the firm regarding whether the firm (or any of
elements.label: text

elements.text: its partners, shareholders or employees while acting as a partner, shareholder or principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: at another firm) has been previously terminated by Freddie Mac or Fannie Mae or had
elements.label: text

elements.text: referrals suspended by Freddie Mac or Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain a certification from the firm that, to the best of the firm’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: knowledge, the firm’s documents have been and continue to be prepared, executed and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: notarized in compliance with applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the firm reports that the firm, its attorneys, notaries or third parties that the firm relies
elements.label: text

elements.text: on to perform any aspect of default-related or REO-related services have previously
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepared, executed or notarized documents that have not been in compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law, the Servicer must conclude that the firm has instituted controls,
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures and processes to address the contributing cause(s) of the firm’s failure to
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with applicable law in order to execute the Servicer Selection Form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac expects Servicers to exercise sound judgment and consider the totality of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances in evaluating the potential legal and reputational risks posed by a firm to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac. The items for consideration outlined above are not intended to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: exhaustive or to disqualify a firm from retention if the Servicer concludes that the firm is
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable considering the totality of the circumstances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Conflicts of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Attorneys must not be affected by a conflict of interest or a potential conflict of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: when handling Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters. The Servicer must retain the most
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualified attorneys in compliance with Freddie Mac requirements to assist with
elements.label: text

elements.text: processing Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters without regard to arrangements that could
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide a financial or personal benefit directly or indirectly to the Servicer, its
elements.label: text

elements.text: employees, outsource companies or third-party vendors used by the Servicer to assist in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing defaulted Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: On the Servicer Selection Form, the Servicer must disclose to Freddie Mac any current,
elements.label: text

elements.text: past (within the last five years) or pending personal and/or financial relationships
elements.label: text

elements.text: between:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer and the firm, including its partners and shareholders (as applicable) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm, including its partners and shareholders (as applicable), and any outsourcing
elements.label: text

elements.text: company or other third-party vendor used by the Servicer to assist in Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: defaulted Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Adverse matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: No substantial part of the firm’s practice can include matters that are adverse to financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: institutions, including Freddie Mac or Fannie Mae. Adverse matters to financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: institutions include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium association, HOA or Cooperative Corporation foreclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consumer debtor or mortgagor representation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bankruptcy trustee representation; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other client(s) that may create a potential conflict of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Disclosure of third-party service providers
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must require the firm to disclose the identity of and relationship with any
elements.label: text

elements.text: entities the firm relies upon to provide third-party support functions performed on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s behalf, including, but not limited to, title searches, title insurance, posting,
elements.label: text

elements.text: publication and process services.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must also require the firm to disclose whether the firm has a process to
elements.label: text

elements.text: select and regularly review costs and performance of vendors of related sources to ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: competitive pricing and high quality.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Management and oversight
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Staff oversight
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the firm has appropriate attorney-to-staff ratios to ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate staff oversight given the size of the firm and the firm’s operational structure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must consider whether the firm practices in a judicial or a non-judicial
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdiction, the firm’s case management practices, the jurisdiction-specific process,
elements.label: text

elements.text: attorney and staff experience, firm technology and firm infrastructure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) File oversight
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the firm has appropriate (i) attorney-to-file and (ii) staff￾to-file ratios, given the size of the firm and the firm’s operational structure. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must take into consideration whether the firm practices in a judicial or a non-judicial
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure jurisdiction, the firm’s case management practices, the jurisdiction-specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: processes, attorney and staff experience, firm technology and firm infrastructure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Employee training
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the firm has written policies for employee training,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including privacy training. When determining whether a firm’s employee training is
elements.label: text

elements.text: adequate, the Servicer must review the frequency of training, the presence of policies and
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures and firm handbooks, manuals and job aids.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the firm has written policies, procedures and/or processes
elements.label: text

elements.text: in place by the date of the submission of the Servicer Selection Form, to ensure the
elements.label: text

elements.text: proper management and supervision of staff and the proper preparation, review,
elements.label: text

elements.text: execution and notarization of default-related documents and REO-related documents. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must also confirm the firm has an escalation process for employees to raise
elements.label: text

elements.text: document execution and other quality control issues to firm management.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain documentation and information related to the firm’s process for
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensuring compliance with its policies, procedures, processes and training, such as an
elements.label: text

elements.text: internal compliance program and/or quality control reviews.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Technology and reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Technology
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the firm has adequate technology in place or
elements.label: text

elements.text: technological capabilities to provide reporting, communication and tracking of key events
elements.label: text

elements.text: and milestones, including access to PACER/ECF or other similar systems to obtain case
elements.label: text

elements.text: and docket information from federal appellate, district and bankruptcy court records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Servicer must confirm that the firm is able to provide status reports and
elements.label: text

elements.text: track significant dates and events for foreclosure, bankruptcy, evictions and REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: closings and has the capability to measure the duration between various process stages, to
elements.label: text

elements.text: identify process impediments (e.g., holds) and to parse holds into different categories.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a firm is multi-jurisdictional or has partnerships or relationships with third parties (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: local counsel, trustee companies or title companies) that will perform or complete some
elements.label: text

elements.text: aspect of the default-related or REO-related work or if the firm relies on other offices to
elements.label: text

elements.text: perform some aspect of the work or provide operational support, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: confirm that the firm maintains a reliable and secure means of exchanging matter
elements.label: text

elements.text: information between each office and any third party the firm relies upon.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must require the firm to describe whether the firm currently uses a universal
elements.label: text

elements.text: translation technology to communicate information between their technological system
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the various Servicers’ systems, or the firm must explain its method for transmitting
elements.label: text

elements.text: information efficiently, accurately and securely to Servicers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Technology staffing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the firm has adequate in-house technical expertise or
elements.label: text

elements.text: readily available vendor support to ensure compliance with Freddie Mac’s automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Capability to report via the Attorney Data Reporting (ADR) System
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the firm has the capability to provide daily reporting to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac of key metrics (i.e., volume, milestones, delays, loss mitigation successes,
elements.label: text

elements.text: litigation detail, etc.) via the ADR System (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools), pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9501.5(b). The Servicer must also ensure that the firm has staff responsible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting data directly to Freddie Mac using the ADR System.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Diversity and communication
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Diversity data
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must confirm that the firm has the capability to report diversity data to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer and Freddie Mac, if necessary.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Referrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is responsible for ensuring that the firm complies with Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements and applicable laws regarding referrals and payment of related fees and
elements.label: text

elements.text: benefits, as further described in Sections 9501.4(b) and 9501.4(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not require the firm to use vendors, outsource companies or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: third-parties specified by the Servicer as a condition of receiving a referral of a Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Default Legal Matter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9501.3: Firm selection and retention (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Selection of firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retention of firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Selection of firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Servicer selects firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that a firm meets the Firm Minimum Requirements specified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9501.2 and all other Guide requirements, then the Servicer must complete and
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit a Servicer Selection Form to Freddie Mac via
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://www.freddiemacsats.com/Security/Login and receive Freddie Mac’s “no
elements.label: text

elements.text: objection” determination before entering into an agreement with a firm to handle Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Default Legal Matters.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac requests additional information from the Servicer as part of this process,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must provide the requested information within the time frame requested by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac. Servicers may not rely upon a previous submission of a Servicer Selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form by another Servicer with respect to a firm that received a “no objection”
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination. Each Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conduct its own due diligence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submit a Servicer Selection Form; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Receive a “no objection” determination for each firm that the Servicer wishes to
elements.label: text

elements.text: retain to handle Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a firm practices in multiple jurisdictions, the Servicer must submit a Servicer Selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form for each jurisdiction for which the Servicer wishes to retain the firm.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Servicer Attorney Tracking System (SATS) registration
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must use SATS, an online process, to submit a Servicer Selection Form to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac for each law firm selected to handle Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters. To
elements.label: text

elements.text: establish access to SATS, Servicers must first register to create a user ID and password at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://www.freddiemacsats.com/Security/Login.
elements.label: text

elements.text: After completing the registration process, SATS will allow users to submit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information required in the Servicer Selection Form to Freddie Mac for review. SATS
elements.label: text

elements.text: will also allow Servicers to respond to Freddie Mac’s requests for additional information,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as necessary, and will allow Servicers to track each submission’s status during the review
elements.label: text

elements.text: process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not review any Servicer Selection Form completed and submitted to
elements.label: text

elements.text: any Freddie Mac e-mail address. Servicers must complete and submit the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selection Form via https://www.freddiemacsats.com/Security/Login.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Freddie Mac review of Servicer Selection Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: After Freddie Mac receives the Servicer Selection Form, Freddie Mac will notify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer via the Servicer’s registered e-mail address with SATS whether Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Objects to the Servicer’s retention of the firm to handle Freddie Mac Default Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has no objection to Servicer’s retention of the firm to handle Freddie Mac Default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Legal Matters; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Needs additional information or documentation or due diligence to be conducted
elements.label: text

elements.text: before deciding whether the firm may be retained. If requested, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide any additional information or documentation to Freddie Mac via
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://www.freddiemacsats.com/Security/Login and must conduct any further due
elements.label: text

elements.text: diligence requested by Freddie Mac within the time period stated in Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Freddie Mac’s response to Servicer firm selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Freddie Mac provides a “no objection” response
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must enter into a contract with the firm (if a contract does not already
elements.label: text

elements.text: exist) as further specified in Section 9501.3(b) to handle Freddie Mac Default Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Matters.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Freddie Mac provides an “objection” response
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines not to retain a particular firm or if Freddie Mac objects to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the retention of a particular firm, the Servicer must notify the firm that the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: cannot be hired for Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) The Servicer decides not to retain firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is not obligated to inform Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer determines that a firm does not meet the Firm Minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer decides not to retain a firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Diversity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are reminded that they must be aware of and comply with Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Sections 1201.10 and 1301.2. The Servicer must commit to practice the
elements.label: text

elements.text: principles of equal employment opportunity and non-discrimination in all its business
elements.label: text

elements.text: activities, including the retention and hiring of firms retained pursuant to this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Retention of firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Servicer contract with firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer has not already entered into a contract with a selected firm and Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac has provided a “no objection” determination, then the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Enter into a contract with the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify Freddie Mac when the contract has been executed by updating SATS via
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://www.freddiemacsats.com/Security/Login, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide a copy of the contract to Freddie Mac, upon request
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Freddie Mac limited retention agreement with firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will enter into a limited retention agreement that sets forth certain key
elements.label: text

elements.text: retention provisions with each selected firm for each jurisdiction in which the firm has
elements.label: text

elements.text: received a “no objection” determination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Conflict between Servicer’s contract and limited retention agreements; Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: respective consent
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer acknowledges that the limited retention agreement recognizes and reflects a
elements.label: text

elements.text: joint attorney-client relationship between the law firm, Freddie Mac and the Servicer, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer consents to such joint representation. The Servicer consents, in advance, to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the selected firm’s representation of Freddie Mac in any Freddie Mac Default Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Matter that is or might be adverse to the Servicer, and the Servicer further agrees that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: firm can use in such representation any information the firm gained in the course of
elements.label: text

elements.text: jointly representing the Servicer and Freddie Mac. In the event of any inconsistency or
elements.label: text

elements.text: conflict between the terms and conditions of the Servicer’s contract with the selected firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the terms and conditions of Freddie Mac’s limited retention agreement with the firm,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s limited retention agreement shall control.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9501.4: Firm training requirements, referrals and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: prohibitions related to Freddie Mac default legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: matters (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Training of firms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Referral of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prohibitions related to Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Training of firms
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Training prior to referral
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not refer any Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to a firm until the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer verifies that the firm has executed a limited retention agreement with Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac and has completed Freddie Mac’s new firm training.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A firm is only required to attend Freddie Mac’s new firm training once, regardless of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: number of Servicers that select and retain the firm.
elements.label: text
